description: Classification of the insolvency and bankruptcy framework in this jurisdiction (e.g., debtor-friendly, creditor-friendly, administrative, judicial). Impacts Loss Given Default (LGD) and recovery rate assumptions.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS), International Financial Reporting Standard 9 (IFRS 9)

description: Standardized code identifying the jurisdiction. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-2/alpha-3 for national jurisdictions, with optional subdivision codes (e.g., US-NY for New York State, EU for European Union).

Hierarchical level of the jurisdiction: national (country-level), sub-national (state/province/region), supranational (multi-country bodies like EU or Basel Committee), or territorial (dependent territories).
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS), Financial Stability Board (FSB)

description: Full legal name of the jurisdiction (e.g., United States of America, State of New York, European Union).
reference: ISO 3166, Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS)

description: Current operational status of the jurisdiction record: active (currently in use), inactive (no longer used for new transactions), historical (archived), or pending (under review for activation).
Timestamp when this jurisdiction record was most recently updated.
The foundational legal system governing the jurisdiction: common law (precedent-based), civil law (code-based), Sharia law (Islamic principles), mixed (combination), customary law, or other.
reference: International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA), Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS)

description: Indicates whether close-out netting provisions in derivative contracts are legally enforceable in this jurisdiction under insolvency or default scenarios. Critical for collateral and credit risk management.
reference: International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA), Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS)

description: Date of the most recent legal opinion confirming netting enforceability in this jurisdiction. Used to track currency of legal assessments.

Name of the primary banking regulatory authority in this jurisdiction (e.g., Federal Reserve System, Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, European Central Bank, Prudential Regulation Authority).
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed), Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC), European Banking Authority (EBA), Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA)

description: Primary banking and financial regulatory frameworks applicable in this jurisdiction (e.g., Basel III, Basel IV, Dodd-Frank Act, MiFID II, MAS Notice 637). Multiple frameworks separated by semicolons.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS), Financial Stability Board (FSB), Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), European Banking Authority (EBA), Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS)

description: Name of the Real-Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) payment system operated in this jurisdiction (e.g., Fedwire, CHAPS, TARGET2). Null if no RTGS system exists.
reference: Real-Time Gross Settlement (RTGS), Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT), Bank for International Settlements (BIS)

description: Indicates whether this jurisdiction is subject to international economic sanctions or embargoes that restrict banking and financial transactions.
reference: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC), United Nations Security Council, European Union Sanctions

description: Name of the primary securities and capital markets regulatory authority in this jurisdiction (e.g., Securities and Exchange Commission, Financial Conduct Authority, European Securities and Markets Authority).
reference: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA), International Organization of Securities Commissions (IOSCO)

description: Abbreviated or commonly used name for the jurisdiction (e.g., USA, New York, EU).
Most recent sovereign credit rating for this jurisdiction from a major rating agency (e.g., AAA, AA+, BBB-). Used for country risk assessment and Probability of Default (PD) modeling.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS), International Organization of Securities Commissions (IOSCO)

description: Name of the rating agency that issued the sovereign credit rating (e.g., Standard & Poor's, Moody's, Fitch Ratings).
Date of the most recent sovereign credit rating assessment.

Mandatory stress testing framework applicable in this jurisdiction: Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR), Dodd-Frank Act Stress Testing (DFAST), European Banking Authority (EBA) stress test, local framework, or none.
reference: Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR), Dodd-Frank Act Stress Testing (DFAST), European Banking Authority (EBA), Federal Reserve System (Fed)

description: Classification of the jurisdiction's tax regime: tax haven (minimal or no taxation), low-tax (preferential tax rates), standard (typical tax rates), or not classified.
reference: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD), Financial Stability Board (FSB)

description: Indicates whether this jurisdiction participates in international tax treaty networks for withholding tax relief and double taxation avoidance.
reference: Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA), Common Reporting Standard (CRS), Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)

description: Primary time zone for this jurisdiction in IANA Time Zone Database format (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo). Used for trade settlement and market hours.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database, Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the counterparty credit rating record in the risk data domain. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) — CRR/Basel III Internal Ratings-Based (IRB) Approach

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of risk for this counterparty. Used for sovereign risk assessment, country limit management, and regulatory capital treatment under Basel III.
reference: BCBS Basel III — Sovereign Risk; ISO 3166-1; OCC Country Risk Management Guidelines

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the Exposure at Default (EAD) amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; BCBS Basel III

description: Standard industry classification code for the counterparty's primary business sector (e.g., GICS, NAICS, SIC). Used for sector concentration risk analysis, portfolio segmentation, and credit policy limits.
reference: GICS — Global Industry Classification Standard; NAICS; SIC; BCBS Basel III — Concentration Risk

description: Reference to the specific legal entity within a counterparty group to which this rating applies, supporting group vs. entity-level rating differentiation.
reference: BCBS Basel III — Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) Standards; FSB LEI Requirements

description: Foreign key linking to collateral.margin_agreement. Business justification: ISDA/CSA margin agreements contain rating trigger clauses that require additional collateral when a counterparty's rating falls below a threshold. Linking counterparty_rating to margin_agreement enabl
Reference to the counterparty, obligor, or issuer entity for whom this credit rating record applies. Links to the counterparty master record.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; OCC Counterparty Credit Risk Guidelines

description: Indicates whether a credit analyst has manually overridden the model-generated rating. True = override applied. Overrides must be documented and approved per credit policy. Supports model performance monitoring and governance.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 — Model Risk Management; OCC Credit Risk Management Guidelines; BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach

description: The Basel III regulatory asset class to which this counterparty is assigned for RWA calculation purposes. Determines the applicable risk weight function and capital treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|sovereign|bank|retail|equity|securitisation|specialised_lending|other — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Articles 147–152; OCC Basel III Final Rule

description: The timestamp when this counterparty rating record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 175 — Data Maintenance; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity

description: The date on which the counterparty was classified as in default per the Basel III definition (CRR Article 178). Null if the counterparty has never defaulted. Used for LGD estimation and historical default analysis.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 178; IFRS 9 — ECL Stage 3; FASB ASC 326 — CECL

description: Indicates whether the counterparty is currently in a state of default as defined under Basel III (CRR Article 178). True = counterparty is in default. Triggers NPL classification, ECL Stage 3, and regulatory reporting.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 178 — Definition of Default; IFRS 9 — Stage 3; FASB ASC 326 — CECL

description: The estimated Exposure at Default (EAD) for this counterparty at the time of rating, expressed in the reporting currency. Represents the total value at risk if the counterparty defaults. Used in RWA and ECL calculations.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 182; IFRS 9 — ECL; Federal Reserve CCAR/DFAST

description: The IFRS 9 / CECL impairment stage assigned to this counterparty: Stage 1 (performing, 12-month ECL), Stage 2 (significant increase in credit risk, lifetime ECL), Stage 3 (credit-impaired, lifetime ECL). Drives provisioning and financial reporting.
reference: IFRS 9 — Financial Instruments (IASB); FASB ASC 326 — CECL; EBA Guidelines on IFRS 9

description: The date from which this rating record becomes effective for credit decisions, RWA calculation, and regulatory reporting purposes.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 175; IFRS 9 — Expected Credit Loss (ECL) Staging

description: The date on which this rating record expires and must be reviewed or renewed. Null for ratings with no defined expiry. Supports rating review cycle management.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 175; OCC Credit Risk Management Guidelines

description: The date of the most recent external agency rating action (S&P, Moody's, or Fitch). Used to assess the currency of external ratings for regulatory and credit purposes.
reference: BCBS Basel III — SA External Credit Assessment; SEC NRSRO Rules

description: The most recent long-term issuer credit rating assigned by Fitch Ratings (e.g., AAA, A+, BBB-, BB). Used for external benchmark comparison, regulatory SA risk weight mapping, and credit underwriting.
reference: Fitch Ratings — Rating Definitions; BCBS Basel III — SA External Credit Assessment; SEC NRSRO Rules

description: The estimated Loss Given Default (LGD) for this counterparty or facility, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.45 = 45%). Represents the proportion of exposure expected to be lost if the counterparty defaults. Used in RWA and ECL calculations.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 181; IFRS 9 — ECL; FASB ASC 326 — CECL

description: The most recent long-term issuer credit rating assigned by Moody's Investors Service (e.g., Aaa, Aa1, Baa2, Ba3). Used for external benchmark comparison, regulatory SA risk weight mapping, and credit underwriting.
reference: Moody's Investors Service — Rating Definitions; BCBS Basel III — SA External Credit Assessment; SEC NRSRO Rules

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this counterparty's credit rating. Supports credit review cycle governance and ensures ratings remain current.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 175; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Credit Risk Management Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the analyst override resulted in an upgrade or downgrade relative to the model-generated rating. Null if no override is in effect. Used for override tracking and model performance analysis.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 — Model Risk Management; OCC Credit Risk Management Guidelines

description: Free-text justification provided by the credit analyst for the rating override. Required when analyst_override_flag is True. Supports audit trail and credit governance review.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 — Model Risk Management; OCC Credit Risk Management Guidelines; BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach

description: The estimated one-year Probability of Default (PD) associated with this internal rating grade, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.00250 = 0.25%). Core input to RWA calculation under the IRB approach and ECL computation under IFRS 9/CECL.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 180; IFRS 9 — ECL; FASB ASC 326 — CECL; Federal Reserve CCAR/DFAST

description: The internal rating grade code assigned to this counterparty immediately prior to the current rating. Enables rating migration analysis and transition matrix construction for credit risk modelling.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; CRR Article 175; IFRS 9 — ECL; Federal Reserve CCAR/DFAST

description: The date on which the prior internal rating grade was assigned. Used in conjunction with prior_rating_code to construct rating migration history and transition matrices.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; CRR Article 175; IFRS 9 — ECL

description: Regulatory capital approach under which this rating is used: Internal Ratings-Based (IRB), Standardized Approach (SA), Foundation IRB (FIRB), or Advanced IRB (AIRB). Determines RWA calculation methodology.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Articles 142–191; OCC Basel III Final Rule

description: The internal credit rating grade code assigned to the counterparty under the bank's Internal Ratings-Based (IRB) model (e.g., 1A, 2B, CG3). Represents the bank's own assessment of creditworthiness.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; OCC Internal Credit Rating Guidelines; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Indicates whether this rating was reviewed and approved by a formal credit rating committee (as opposed to individual analyst approval). True = committee-approved. Required for large or complex exposures per credit policy.
reference: OCC Credit Risk Management Guidelines; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach

description: The date on which the current internal credit rating was formally assigned or last affirmed by the credit analyst or rating committee. Represents the principal business event date for this rating record.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; CRR Article 175 — Data Maintenance

description: Human-readable description of the internal rating grade (e.g., 'Investment Grade — Strong', 'Sub-Investment Grade — Speculative'). Provides qualitative context for the rating code.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; Federal Reserve SR 11-7 Model Risk Management

description: The name or code of the credit rating methodology applied to derive this rating (e.g., 'Corporate Scorecard v3.2', 'Financial Institution Model v2.1', 'Sovereign Rating Framework'). Supports model governance and audit trail.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 — Model Risk Management; OCC Model Risk Management; BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach

description: The version identifier of the rating model or scorecard used to produce this rating (e.g., 'v3.2', '2024.1'). Supports model lifecycle management, backtesting, and SR 11-7 model inventory compliance.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 — Model Risk Management; OCC Model Risk Management Guidelines

description: The rating outlook indicating the likely direction of the credit rating over the medium term (12–24 months). Applies to both internal and external ratings. Values: positive, stable, negative, developing.
reference: S&P Global Ratings — Outlook Definitions; Moody's — Rating Outlook; Fitch — Rating Outlook; BCBS Basel III

description: Current lifecycle status of the rating record. 'active' indicates the rating is in force; 'under_review' indicates a rating action is pending; 'withdrawn' indicates the rating has been formally removed; 'override' indicates an analyst override is in effect.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Credit Risk Management Guidelines

description: The business event that triggered this rating action or review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual_review|financial_update|covenant_breach|market_event|acquisition|analyst_initiated|regulatory_requirement|credit_deterioration|other — promote to reference product]
reference: OCC Credit Risk Management Guidelines; BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 175; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Classifies whether this rating applies to the obligor/counterparty as a whole, a specific facility, an issuer, or a specific debt issue. Determines the scope of the rating assignment.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; CRR Article 170 — Rating Systems

description: The regulatory risk weight percentage assigned to this counterparty under the applicable Basel III approach (IRB or SA), expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.75 = 75%). Directly drives Risk-Weighted Assets (RWA) calculation for capital adequacy.
reference: BCBS Basel III — CRR Articles 147–191; OCC Basel III Final Rule; Federal Reserve Reg Q

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this rating record originated (e.g., 'MOODYS_RISKAUTH', 'SAS_RISK', 'LIQ_IQ'). Supports data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and audit trail.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; SOX Section 404 — Data Lineage

description: The most recent long-term issuer credit rating assigned by S&P Global Ratings (e.g., AAA, AA+, BBB-, BB). Used for external benchmark comparison, regulatory SA risk weight mapping, and credit underwriting.
reference: S&P Global Ratings — Rating Definitions; BCBS Basel III — SA External Credit Assessment; SEC NRSRO Rules

description: The timestamp when this counterparty rating record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data quality monitoring.
reference: SOX Section 404; BCBS Basel III — CRR Article 175 — Data Maintenance; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity

description: Indicates whether the counterparty's rating is currently on a rating watch list, and the direction of the potential rating action. 'none' indicates no active watch. Signals near-term rating change risk.
reference: S&P Global Ratings — CreditWatch; Moody's — Rating Review; Fitch — Rating Watch; BCBS Basel III

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each credit exposure measurement record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the credit_exposure data product.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) — CRE framework

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the counterparty's country of domicile or incorporation. Used for geographic concentration risk analysis and sovereign risk weighting under CRR2 Article 395.

Reference to the ISDA/CSA netting set under which this exposure is netted for counterparty credit risk purposes. Drives netting benefit calculations for EAD and CVA.
reference: ISDA Master Agreement; BCBS CRE53 — SA-CCR Netting Sets; CRR2 Article 272

reference: BCBS CRE20 — Counterparty Credit Risk; OCC Counterparty Credit Risk Guidance

Foreign key linking to treasury.repo_position. Business justification: Credit exposure calculations incorporate repo positions for counterparty credit risk measurement and CVA calculation under Basel III SA-CCR. Repo positions are collateralized exposures requiring speci

Foreign key linking to risk.risk_ecl_provision. Business justification: credit_exposure embeds ecl_provision (DECIMAL) and ecl_stage (STRING) as denormalized values. risk_ecl_provision is the authoritative ECL provisioning record under IFRS 9/CECL. Adding risk_ecl_provisi

Foreign key linking to risk.risk_limit. Business justification: Credit exposures are monitored against counterparty limits, country limits, and portfolio limits. The credit_exposure table has large_exposure_flag but no reference to the specific limit being monitor

Current market value of eligible financial collateral posted against this exposure after applicable haircuts under the Financial Collateral Comprehensive Method (FCCM). Reduces EAD and RWA.
reference: BCBS CRE22 — Credit Risk Mitigation; CRR2 Articles 197-217; BCBS Haircut Framework

description: Herfindahl-Hirschman Index score measuring the degree of concentration of this exposure within its sector, geography, or single-name bucket. Used for concentration risk monitoring and regulatory large exposure limit compliance.
reference: BCBS Concentration Risk Guidelines; CRR2 Article 395 — Large Exposures; EBA GL/2021/07

description: Classification of the counterparty for regulatory capital and large exposure reporting purposes. Determines applicable risk weight under SA and eligibility for IRB treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|financial_institution|sovereign|central_bank|retail|sme|fund — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS CRE20; CRR2 Article 112; FINRA Counterparty Classification

description: Timestamp when this credit exposure record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under SOX and Basel Pillar 3.
reference: SOX Section 404; BCBS BCBS239 — Risk Data Aggregation and Reporting

description: Current mark-to-market (MTM) replacement cost of the exposure as of the measurement date, before netting and collateral. For derivatives, represents the positive MTM value. For loans, represents the outstanding principal balance.
reference: BCBS CRE53 — SA-CCR Current Exposure; ISDA Credit Support Annex; Murex MTM Module

description: Market value of counterparty credit risk embedded in OTC derivative positions, representing the expected loss due to counterparty default. Calculated under SA-CVA or BA-CVA regulatory framework. Impacts P&L and regulatory capital.
reference: BCBS CRE51 — CVA Risk Framework; CRR2 Article 381; IFRS 13 Fair Value; ISDA CVA Standard

description: Regulatory methodology used to calculate the CVA capital charge: Standardised Approach for CVA (SA-CVA), Basic Approach for CVA (BA-CVA), or exempted (for transactions with non-financial counterparties below threshold).
reference: BCBS CRE51 — CVA Framework; CRR2 Articles 382-386

description: Regulatory capital requirement for CVA risk calculated under SA-CVA or BA-CVA methodology as prescribed by BCBS CRE51. Represents the RWA equivalent for counterparty credit valuation adjustment risk.
reference: BCBS CRE51 — SA-CVA/BA-CVA Capital Charge; CRR2 Article 383; Basel III Final Rule

description: Adjustment to derivative fair value reflecting the bank's own credit risk, representing the benefit to the bank from its own potential default. Reported under IFRS 13 but excluded from regulatory CVA capital charge.
reference: IFRS 13 — Fair Value Measurement; BCBS CRE51; ISDA DVA Standard

description: Estimated total exposure amount outstanding at the time of default, expressed in the reporting currency. Incorporates credit conversion factors (CCF) for off-balance-sheet items and SA-CCR replacement cost for derivatives. Core input to RWA and ECL calculation.
reference: BCBS CRE36.88 — EAD Estimation; CRR2 Article 166; SA-CCR CRE52

description: Effective maturity parameter (M) in years as defined under the IRB Advanced approach, capped at 5 years and floored at 1 year. Adjusts the IRB capital formula for longer-dated exposures via the maturity adjustment factor.
reference: BCBS CRE36.93 — Effective Maturity; CRR2 Article 162

description: Average exposure across all simulation paths at a future time horizon, used as the basis for CVA calculation and expected credit loss (ECL) provisioning under IFRS 9.
reference: BCBS CRE53 — Expected Exposure Profile; IFRS 9 ECL; SA-CVA CRE51

description: Regulatory asset class of the exposure under Basel III/CRR2 framework, determining the applicable risk weight and capital treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|sovereign|institution|retail|equity|securitisation|other — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS CRE20 — Standardised Approach; CRR2 Article 112; Basel III Final Rule

description: Externally-known business reference number for this credit exposure record, as assigned by the risk management platform (SAS Risk Management / Moodys RiskAuthority) or trading system (Murex/Calypso). Used for reconciliation and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAS Risk Management — Exposure Reference; Murex Credit Risk Module

description: Current lifecycle status of the credit exposure. Drives provisioning, regulatory classification, and workout processes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|closed|defaulted|restructured|watch_list|written_off — promote to reference product if additional states required]
reference: BCBS CRE — Credit Risk Classification; IFRS 9 Stage Classification; OCC Loan Classification Guidelines

description: Adjustment to derivative fair value reflecting the cost or benefit of funding uncollateralised derivative positions. Captures the funding spread between the bank's borrowing cost and the risk-free rate.
reference: IFRS 13 — Fair Value Measurement; ISDA FVA Framework; Basel III Funding Cost

description: Exchange rate applied to translate the exposure from its original currency to the reporting currency as of the measurement date. Sourced from the bank's official FX rate feed.
reference: BCBS FX Risk; CRR2 Article 352; ECB Reference Rates

description: Indicates whether this exposure meets or exceeds the large exposure threshold (10% of eligible capital) requiring regulatory reporting under CRR2 Article 395. Triggers enhanced monitoring and reporting obligations.
reference: CRR2 Article 395 — Large Exposures Limit; EBA GL/2021/07; BCBS Large Exposures Standard

description: Estimated fraction of the Exposure at Default (EAD) that would be lost in the event of default, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.45 = 45%). Reflects collateral, seniority, and recovery assumptions under IRB Advanced approach.
reference: BCBS CRE36.83 — LGD Estimation; IFRS 9 ECL; CECL ASC 326; OCC LGD Guidance

description: Contractual maturity date of the underlying credit facility or instrument. Used to calculate effective maturity (M) for IRB capital calculation and to determine the exposure's remaining tenor for PFE and CVA profiles.
reference: BCBS CRE36.93 — Effective Maturity; CRR2 Article 162; Finastra Loan IQ Maturity Date

description: The as-of date on which the credit exposure was measured and calculated. Represents the principal business event date for this record. Used for point-in-time regulatory reporting (CCAR, DFAST, Pillar 3).
reference: BCBS CCAR/DFAST Stress Testing Guidelines; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Credit exposure after applying netting agreements and eligible collateral offsets (EAD minus collateral value). Represents the bank's actual economic risk position used for limit monitoring and large exposure reporting.
reference: CRR2 Article 395 — Large Exposures; BCBS CRE22 — Netting and Collateral; ISDA Netting Opinion

description: Estimated probability that the obligor will default within a one-year horizon, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.00250 = 0.25%). Core IRB parameter used in RWA and ECL calculation under IFRS 9 and CECL.
reference: BCBS CRE36.46 — PD Estimation; IFRS 9 — Expected Credit Loss; CECL ASC 326

description: Indicates whether this exposure record is included in the bank's Pillar 3 public disclosure reporting under Basel III. Drives the regulatory reporting extract for AxiomSL/Wolters Kluwer OneSumX.
reference: BCBS Pillar 3 Disclosure Requirements (January 2015, updated 2018); CRR2 Article 431

description: Estimated maximum exposure at a specified confidence level (typically 95%) over the remaining life of the transaction, capturing future market movements. Used in counterparty credit limit monitoring and CVA calculation.
reference: BCBS CRE53 — SA-CCR PFE Add-on; ISDA SIMM; Murex/Calypso PFE Engine

description: Regulatory methodology applied to calculate risk-weighted assets (RWA) for this exposure: Standardised Approach (SA), Foundation Internal Ratings-Based (F-IRB), Advanced IRB (A-IRB), Standardised Approach for Counterparty Credit Risk (SA-CCR), or Internal Model Method (IMM).
reference: BCBS CRE — IRB Approach; CRR2 Articles 143-191; Basel III Final Rule

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the bank's reporting currency into which all exposure amounts are translated for regulatory capital and Pillar 3 reporting (typically USD or EUR).
reference: ISO 4217; BCBS Pillar 3 Disclosure Requirements; CRR2 Article 430

description: Risk weight percentage applied to the exposure to derive credit RWA, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.00 = 100%). Determined by exposure class, counterparty rating, and regulatory approach (SA or IRB).
reference: BCBS CRE20 — SA Risk Weights; CRR2 Articles 114-134; Basel III Final Rule

description: Risk-weighted asset amount for credit risk calculated for this exposure under the applicable regulatory approach (SA or IRB). Primary input to CET1 capital ratio calculation and Pillar 3 disclosure.
reference: BCBS CRE — RWA Calculation; CRR2 Article 92; Basel III Final Rule; CCAR/DFAST Capital Planning

description: Operational system of record from which this credit exposure record was sourced (e.g., Murex/Calypso for derivatives, Finastra Loan IQ for loans, SAS Risk Management for aggregated risk). Supports data lineage and BCBS 239 compliance.
reference: BCBS 239 — Risk Data Aggregation and Reporting Principles; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp when this credit exposure record was last modified or recalculated. Tracks recalibration events, model updates, and data corrections.
reference: SOX Section 404; BCBS239 — Risk Data Aggregation

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each ECL provisioning and credit quality classification record in the risk domain. Primary key for the risk_ecl_provision data product.

Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: IFRS 9 and CECL period-end ECL provision reporting requires provisions to be attributed to specific accounting periods for financial statement preparation and regulatory disclosure. reporting_date exi

Foreign key linking to collateral.collateral_valuation. Business justification: IFRS9 ECL LGD estimation requires the current collateral valuation to compute recoverable amounts. risk_ecl_provision.collateral_value is a denormalized copy of collateral_valuation.net_collateral_val

Foreign key linking to risk.counterparty_rating. Business justification: ECL provisions use PD/LGD estimates from counterparty ratings. The risk_ecl_provision table has internal_credit_rating, external_credit_rating, rating_agency fields which duplicate data from counterpa
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the provision amount, EAD, and ECL figures are denominated. Supports multi-currency provisioning and FX translation for consolidated reporting.
reference: ISO 4217, IFRS 9, FASB ASC 830 (Foreign Currency Matters)

description: Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: IFRS 9 audit trail and financial statement reconciliation require ECL provisions to reference the specific GL impairment/provision account. While journal_entry_id exists, a direct GL account reference

Reference to the underlying loan or credit facility for which the ECL provision is calculated. Links to the loan master record in the Core Banking System (Temenos T24 / FIS Profile) or Loan Origination System (Finastra Fusion Loan IQ).
Reference to the borrower or counterparty customer record associated with this ECL provision. Enables customer-level aggregation of credit risk exposure and provision balances.

Foreign key linking to collateral.pledge. Business justification: IFRS9/CECL ECL calculations require collateral coverage assessment. LGD estimates depend on pledged collateral value, haircuts, and enforceability. Essential for Stage 2/3 provision calculations and r

Foreign key linking to reference.product_type. Business justification: IFRS 9 and CECL provisioning is performed by product type/portfolio segment — mortgage provisions differ from credit card or corporate loan provisions. Regulatory ECL disclosures (FINREP, Pillar 3) re

Foreign key linking to risk.stress_scenario. Business justification: ECL provisions are calculated under multiple macroeconomic scenarios (base, upside, downside) per IFRS 9 requirements. The risk_ecl_provision table has macro_scenario_type field which should be replac

Foreign key linking to risk.stress_test_run. Business justification: risk_ecl_provision links to stress_scenario (EXISTS) but not to the specific stress_test_run execution. Under CCAR/DFAST, stressed ECL provisions are computed per run execution. Adding stress_test_run

business_glossary_term: Current Expected Credit Loss (CECL) Pool Classification
description: FASB ASC 326 (CECL) pool or segment classification used for collective assessment of expected credit losses. Identifies the homogeneous risk pool to which the instrument is assigned for CECL measurement purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial_real_estate|commercial_industrial|consumer_mortgage|consumer_auto|consumer_unsecured|credit_card|other — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the ECL provision was calculated on a collective (pooled) basis rather than an individual (specific) basis. Collective assessment is applied when individual assessment is not practicable, typically for homogeneous retail portfolios.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraph B5.5.1, FASB ASC 326-20-30-2, OCC Bulletin 2020-49

description: Timestamp when this ECL provision record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404, BCBS BCBS239 (RDARR), GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates the cure or exit status of a previously defaulted or forborne exposure. Tracks whether the borrower has exited NPL/forbearance status, is in the probation period, has been fully cured, or has re-defaulted.
reference: EBA NPL Guidelines EBA/GL/2018/06, IFRS 9 paragraph 5.5.11

description: Number of calendar days the credit obligation is past its contractual due date as of the reporting date. Key trigger for stage migration (>30 DPD for Stage 2, >90 DPD for Stage 3 / NPL under EBA definition) and NPL classification.
reference: EBA NPL Definition EBA/GL/2016/07, BCBS Basel III paragraph 452, OCC Loan Classification

description: Indicates whether the obligor is in default as defined under Basel III IRB framework (>90 DPD or unlikely-to-pay criteria). Triggers Stage 3 classification under IFRS 9 and NPL status under EBA definition.
reference: Basel III IRB paragraph 452, IFRS 9 paragraph B5.5.37, EBA NPL Guidelines

description: The effective interest rate used to discount future expected credit loss cash flows to present value as of the reporting date. Per IFRS 9, this is the original effective interest rate (or credit-adjusted EIR for POCI assets).

Estimated gross exposure of the credit facility at the point of default, expressed in the instrument currency. Represents the outstanding balance plus expected drawdowns on undrawn commitments used as the base for ECL calculation.
reference: Basel III IRB paragraph 474, IFRS 9 paragraph B5.5.29, BCBS d424

description: Calculated 12-month ECL provision amount representing the probability-weighted credit loss from default events expected within 12 months of the reporting date. Applied to Stage 1 exposures. Computed as PD_12month × LGD × EAD discounted at the effective interest rate.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraph 5.5.5, FASB ASC 326-20-30, OCC Bulletin 2020-49

description: Calculated lifetime ECL provision amount representing the probability-weighted credit loss over the remaining life of the instrument. Applied to Stage 2 and Stage 3 exposures under IFRS 9 and all exposures under CECL.

Indicates whether the credit exposure has been subject to forbearance measures (concessions granted to a borrower experiencing financial difficulty). Forbearance status affects stage classification and NPL reporting under EBA guidelines.
reference: EBA Forbearance Guidelines EBA/GL/2018/06, IFRS 9 paragraph B5.5.26

description: IFRS 9 credit impairment stage classification: Stage 1 (performing, 12-month ECL), Stage 2 (significant increase in credit risk, lifetime ECL), Stage 3 (credit-impaired, lifetime ECL). Drives the ECL measurement horizon and provision methodology.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraphs 5.5.3–5.5.5, EBA Guidelines on credit risk management EBA/GL/2017/06

description: Estimated proportion of the EAD that would be lost in the event of default, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.45 = 45%). Reflects collateral coverage, seniority, and recovery expectations. Key input to ECL and IRB RWA calculation.
reference: Basel III IRB paragraph 468, IFRS 9 paragraph B5.5.29, BCBS d424

description: Ratio of the outstanding loan balance to the current collateral value, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.75 = 75% LTV). Key risk indicator for secured lending provisions, particularly mortgage portfolios. Influences LGD and stage classification.
reference: Basel III CRR Article 124, OCC Comptrollers Handbook — Real Estate Lending, BCBS d424

description: Forward-looking macroeconomic adjustment factor applied to the base ECL model output to incorporate point-in-time economic conditions (e.g., GDP growth, unemployment rate, house price index scenarios). Reflects IFRS 9 requirement for unbiased, probability-weighted forward-looking information.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraphs 5.5.17, B5.5.49–B5.5.54, EBA Guidelines EBA/GL/2020/06

description: Probability weight assigned to the macroeconomic scenario (base, upside, downside) used in the probability-weighted ECL calculation. The sum of scenario weights across all scenarios for a given exposure must equal 1.0.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraph B5.5.41, EBA Guidelines EBA/GL/2020/06, CCAR/DFAST scenario framework

description: Contractual maturity date of the credit instrument. Determines the remaining life used in lifetime ECL calculation and the PD term structure horizon for Stage 2 and Stage 3 provisions.

Regulatory asset quality classification of the credit exposure per OCC and EBA NPL frameworks: performing, watch list, substandard, doubtful, or loss. Drives regulatory capital and provisioning requirements under the standardized approach.
reference: OCC Comptrollers Handbook — Loan Classification, EBA NPL Guidelines EBA/GL/2018/06, Basel III SA

description: Date on which the credit exposure was first classified as non-performing under the EBA NPL definition. Used for NPL vintage analysis, cure period tracking, and EBA NPL ratio reporting.
reference: EBA NPL Guidelines EBA/GL/2016/07, EBA NPL Transaction Templates

description: Date on which the credit facility was originally originated or the financial instrument was initially recognized. Used to determine the initial recognition PD for SICR assessment and POCI identification under IFRS 9.

Estimated probability that the obligor will default within the next 12 months, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.00250 = 0.25%). Used for Stage 1 ECL calculation and IRB capital requirement computation.
reference: Basel III IRB paragraph 461, IFRS 9 paragraph B5.5.28, BCBS d424

description: Estimated probability that the obligor will default over the remaining contractual life of the instrument. Used for Stage 2 and Stage 3 lifetime ECL calculation under IFRS 9.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraphs 5.5.3, B5.5.28, EBA Guidelines EBA/GL/2017/06

description: Indicates whether the financial instrument was purchased or originated as credit-impaired (POCI). POCI assets use a credit-adjusted effective interest rate and lifetime ECL from initial recognition under IFRS 9.

High-level portfolio segment classification of the credit exposure for ECL modeling and regulatory reporting purposes. Determines the applicable IRB sub-model (retail, corporate, sovereign, etc.) and CECL pool assignment.
reference: Basel III IRB paragraphs 231–234, IFRS 9, FASB ASC 326-20-30-2

description: The recognized ECL provision balance recorded on the balance sheet as of the reporting date. Equals ecl_12month_amount for Stage 1 or ecl_lifetime_amount for Stage 2/3. This is the gross provision before any write-offs.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraph 5.5.1, FASB ASC 326-20-35, IASB ED/2013/3

description: Net change in the provision balance during the reporting period (current period provision minus prior period provision). Positive values indicate an increase (charge to P&L); negative values indicate a release (credit to P&L). Used for income statement impairment charge reporting.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraph 5.5.8, FASB ASC 326-20-35-8, IASB ED/2013/3

description: Externally-known business identifier for the ECL provision record, used for cross-system reconciliation and regulatory reporting. Typically sourced from the Risk Management Platform (SAS Risk Management / Moodys RiskAuthority).
Current lifecycle status of the ECL provision record. Indicates whether the provision is active, has been reversed upon cure, transferred to another stage, written off, or is under review.

Estimated amount expected to be recovered from a written-off or defaulted exposure through collateral realization, guarantees, or workout proceedings. Informs the net LGD estimate and provision adequacy assessment.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraph B5.5.29, Basel III IRB paragraph 468, OCC Comptrollers Handbook

description: The balance sheet or reporting reference date as of which the ECL provision is measured and staged. Represents the principal business event date for this record, used for period-end regulatory and financial reporting.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraph 5.5.3, FASB ASC 326-20-30, OCC Bulletin 2020-49

business_glossary_term: Significant Increase in Credit Risk (SICR) Flag
description: Indicates whether a significant increase in credit risk has been identified for this exposure since initial recognition, triggering migration from Stage 1 to Stage 2 under IFRS 9. Based on quantitative and qualitative SICR criteria.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraphs 5.5.3, 5.5.9–5.5.11, B5.5.1–B5.5.22, EBA Guidelines EBA/GL/2017/06

description: Date on which the most recent IFRS 9 stage migration occurred (e.g., Stage 1 to Stage 2, Stage 2 to Stage 3, or back-migration). Supports stage migration analysis, provision volatility monitoring, and audit trail requirements.
Timestamp when this ECL provision record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements for regulatory reporting.
reference: SOX Section 404, BCBS BCBS239 (RDARR), GDPR Article 5

description: Cumulative amount of the credit exposure that has been written off against the provision balance, reducing both the gross loan balance and the provision. Triggered when there is no reasonable expectation of recovery.
reference: IFRS 9 paragraph 5.4.4, FASB ASC 326-20-35-8, OCC Loan Classification

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each risk factor record in the enterprise risk factor master registry. Serves as the primary key for all downstream model linkages and risk calculations.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) — FRTB SA/IMA Risk Factor Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code associated with the risk factor (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). For FX risk factors, this is the base currency. For interest rate factors, this is the currency of the underlying rate curve. Null for non-currency-denominated factors such as equity indices.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; BCBS FRTB FX Risk Class Definition

description: Foreign key linking to reference.holiday_calendar. Business justification: FRTB RFET requires 250 trading-day observation windows; risk factor observation counts depend on the applicable market holiday calendar. Linking factor to holiday_calendar enables accurate trading-day

Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: FRTB and Basel IV risk factor classification is jurisdiction-specific — EU CRR3 and US Basel III endgame have different modellability and bucketing rules. Linking factors to jurisdiction enables juris

Foreign key linking to reference.rate_benchmark. Business justification: FRTB risk factor assignment and VaR model calibration require linking risk factors to their underlying benchmark rate (SOFR, EURIBOR, etc.). Market risk quants use this to map rate sensitivity factors

Foreign key linking to risk.stress_scenario. Business justification: Risk factors are shocked under stress scenarios. The risk_factor table has stress_period_start_date, stress_period_end_date, ccar_scenario_flag — these link to stress_scenario definitions. N:1 relatio

Broad asset class to which the risk factor belongs under the FRTB framework. Determines the applicable sensitivity-based method (SBM) bucket, risk weight, and correlation parameters for regulatory capital computation.
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325 — Asset Class Definitions; OCC Market Risk Rule

description: Statistical methodology used to calibrate the risk factor's shock distribution and volatility parameters for VaR, stressed VaR, and ECL models. Governs model validation requirements and regulatory approval under IRB and IMA frameworks.
reference: SR 11-7 Model Risk Management; BCBS FRTB IMA — Calibration Standards; IFRS 9 ECL Model Requirements

business_glossary_term: Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR) Scenario Flag
description: Indicates whether this risk factor is included in the Federal Reserve's CCAR/DFAST stress testing scenario framework. True for factors that are shocked in supervisory adverse and severely adverse scenarios. Governs inclusion in stress testing data submissions to the Federal Reserve.
reference: Federal Reserve CCAR Instructions; Dodd-Frank Act Stress Testing (DFAST) Requirements; Fed SR 15-18

description: Timestamp when this risk factor record was first created in the enterprise risk factor registry. Provides the audit trail entry point for data lineage and regulatory examination purposes. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: BCBS 239 — Data Lineage and Audit Trail; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: Credit quality classification of the reference entity or issuer underlying a credit spread risk factor. Determines the applicable FRTB risk weight and correlation parameters. 'investment_grade' corresponds to IG-rated issuers (BBB- and above); 'sub_investment_grade' to HY/NR issuers.
reference: BCBS FRTB — CSR Risk Weights by Credit Quality; Basel III IRB Credit Quality Steps; S&P/Moody's Rating Scales

description: Date from which this risk factor definition became effective and was approved for use in production risk models. Marks the start of the factor's active lifecycle in the enterprise model inventory.
reference: SR 11-7 Model Lifecycle Management; BCBS 239 Data Governance

description: Date on which this risk factor definition expires or is scheduled for retirement from production models. Null for open-ended factors with no planned expiry. Used for model lifecycle management and proactive replacement planning (e.g., LIBOR cessation dates).
reference: SR 11-7 Model Lifecycle Management; FSOC LIBOR Transition Timeline

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying the risk factor across systems (e.g., 'IR_USD_SOFR_3M', 'FX_EURUSD', 'EQ_SPX'). Used as the canonical business identifier in model configurations, regulatory submissions, and cross-system references.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Risk Factor Eligibility Test (RFEТ); SAS Risk Management Factor Registry

description: Free-text narrative description of the risk factor, including its economic interpretation, model usage context, known limitations, and any special handling instructions. Supports model documentation requirements under SR 11-7 and provides context for risk analysts and model validators.
reference: SR 11-7 — Model Documentation Standards; BCBS 239 Metadata Requirements

description: Full descriptive name of the risk factor as used in business communications, model documentation, and regulatory reports (e.g., 'USD SOFR 3-Month Swap Rate', 'EUR/USD Spot FX Rate', 'S&P 500 Equity Index').
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325; ISDA Risk Factor Taxonomy

description: Current lifecycle status of the risk factor in the enterprise model inventory. 'active' factors are used in live production models; 'deprecated' factors are retained for historical audit; 'under_review' factors are undergoing model validation or regulatory review.
reference: SR 11-7 — Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management; OCC Model Risk Guidance

description: Primary classification of the risk factor by its economic nature. Drives model selection, regulatory capital treatment, and VaR/CVA calculation methodology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: interest_rate|fx_rate|equity|commodity|credit_spread|volatility|inflation|real_estate|pd|lgd|ead — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325 — Risk Factor Classification; Basel III Market Risk Framework

business_glossary_term: Fundamental Review of the Trading Book (FRTB) Bucket
description: FRTB Sensitivity-Based Method (SBM) bucket assignment for this risk factor as defined in BCBS FRTB Article 325. Determines the applicable risk weight and intra-bucket/inter-bucket correlation parameters for regulatory capital computation (e.g., 'GIRR_Bucket_1', 'CSR_non_sec_Bucket_3', 'EQ_Bucket_7').
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325 — SBM Bucket Definitions; EBA ITS on FRTB SA

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code indicating the geographic jurisdiction of the risk factor (e.g., 'USA' for US rates, 'GBR' for UK gilts, 'EMU' for Eurozone). Used for geographic concentration analysis, country risk reporting, and regulatory capital bucketing.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; BCBS FRTB — Country Risk Buckets; EBA Country Risk Reporting

description: Regulatory or internal holding period in calendar days used for VaR and Expected Shortfall (ES) calculations for this risk factor (e.g., 10 days for market risk under Basel, 1 day for internal daily VaR). Determines the time horizon over which potential losses are measured.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Liquidity Horizon Requirements; Basel III Market Risk — 10-Day VaR; ISDA SIMM

business_glossary_term: International Financial Reporting Standard 9 (IFRS 9) Expected Credit Loss (ECL) Flag
description: Indicates whether this risk factor is used as a macroeconomic variable or credit parameter in IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss (ECL) models. True for factors such as GDP growth, unemployment rate, PD term structures, and LGD curves used in forward-looking ECL projections.
reference: IFRS 9 — Impairment and ECL Model Requirements; IASB IFRS 9 Application Guidance; CECL ASC 326

description: Indicates whether this risk factor is used as a proxy for another illiquid or unobservable factor. True when the factor substitutes for a non-modellable risk factor (NMRF) in model calculations. Proxy relationships require additional model validation documentation under SR 11-7.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Non-Modellable Risk Factor (NMRF) Treatment; SR 11-7 Model Risk Management

description: Most recent date on which a real market price observation was recorded for this risk factor. Used in RFET assessment to verify that the factor has sufficient recent observations to qualify as modellable under FRTB IMA. Critical for daily data quality monitoring.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Risk Factor Eligibility Test (RFET); BCBS 239 Data Quality Standards

description: Date of the most recent independent model validation review for this risk factor's calibration and methodology. Required under SR 11-7 to ensure ongoing model fitness. Triggers re-validation workflow when the elapsed period exceeds the validation frequency threshold.
reference: SR 11-7 — Independent Model Validation Requirements; OCC Model Risk Guidance 2011-12

description: FRTB-prescribed liquidity horizon in days assigned to this risk factor, reflecting the time required to exit or hedge the risk position in stressed market conditions (e.g., 10, 20, 40, 60, or 120 days per FRTB asset class). Used in ES scaling for IMA capital calculations.
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325 — Liquidity Horizon Categories; EBA ITS on FRTB IMA Liquidity Horizons

description: Name of the primary market data vendor or internal source from which this risk factor's time series is sourced (e.g., 'Bloomberg', 'Refinitiv', 'ICE Data Services', 'Federal Reserve H.15', 'Internal Pricing Model'). Governs data lineage and RFEТ (Risk Factor Eligibility Test) compliance under FRTB IMA.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Risk Factor Eligibility Test (RFET); BCBS 239 Data Lineage Requirements

description: Vendor-specific ticker or field identifier used to retrieve this risk factor's market data from the designated source system (e.g., Bloomberg ticker 'USGG10YR Index', Refinitiv RIC '.SPX'). Enables automated data feed mapping and reconciliation.
reference: Bloomberg Data License Standards; Refinitiv Data Model; BCBS 239 RDARR

description: Scheduled date for the next independent model validation review of this risk factor. Computed based on the validation frequency policy (e.g., annual for Tier 1 models, biennial for Tier 2). Drives the model validation calendar and resource planning.
reference: SR 11-7 — Model Validation Frequency; OCC Model Risk Guidance 2011-12

description: Length of the historical data observation window in calendar days used for calibrating this risk factor's shock distribution (e.g., 250 days for 1-year VaR, 1260 days for 5-year stressed VaR). Governs the backtesting and RFET assessment period.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Historical Observation Period Requirements; SR 11-7 Model Validation

description: Factor code of the observable proxy risk factor used when is_proxy_factor is True. Documents the substitution relationship for model validation, audit, and NMRF capital add-on calculations. Null when the factor is directly observable.
reference: BCBS FRTB — NMRF Proxy Methodology; SR 11-7 Model Documentation Requirements

description: FRTB IMA classification of the risk factor as 'modellable' (RFET passed — sufficient real price observations) or 'non_modellable' (NMRF — requires stressed scenario capital add-on). Directly impacts IMA capital calculation and must be reported to regulators. 'not_applicable' for SA-only factors.
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325 — Risk Factor Eligibility Test (RFET); EBA ITS on FRTB IMA; OCC Market Risk Rule

business_glossary_term: Risk Factor Eligibility Test (RFET) Observation Count
description: Number of real price observations recorded for this risk factor over the RFET assessment period (typically 12 months). A minimum of 24 observations (or 100 for the alternative test) is required for the factor to be classified as modellable under FRTB IMA. Populated by the market data quality process.
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325 — RFET Minimum Observation Thresholds; EBA ITS on FRTB IMA

description: Enterprise risk classification indicating which risk management pillar this factor belongs to: market risk (VaR, CVA), credit risk (PD/LGD/EAD, ECL, RWA), operational risk (SMA), or liquidity risk (LCR, NSFR). Governs routing to the appropriate risk engine and regulatory reporting stream.
reference: Basel III Pillar 1 — Market, Credit, and Operational Risk; BCBS 239 RDARR

description: Industry sector classification of the risk factor's underlying issuer or reference entity (e.g., 'financials', 'energy', 'technology', 'sovereign', 'covered_bond'). Applies primarily to credit spread and equity risk factors. Aligns with FRTB SBM sector buckets for correlation and risk weight assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financials|energy|technology|sovereign|covered_bond|consumer_discretionary|industrials|utilities|real_estate|materials — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325 — CSR and Equity Sector Buckets; GICS Sector Classification

description: Convention used to express risk factor shocks in sensitivity and scenario analyses: 'absolute' (additive shift in units), 'relative' (percentage change), 'log_return' (log-normal return), or 'basis_point' (1bp = 0.0001 shift for rates). Determines how sensitivities (delta, vega) are computed and reported.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Sensitivity Calculation Standards; ISDA SIMM Shock Conventions

description: End date of the identified historical stress period used for stressed VaR and FRTB stressed ES calibration. Together with stress_period_start_date, defines the calibration window that produces the most adverse 12-month stressed scenario for this risk factor.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Stressed ES Calibration; Basel II.5 Stressed VaR Requirements; Fed DFAST Stress Scenarios

description: Start date of the identified historical stress period used for stressed VaR (sVaR) and FRTB stressed ES calibration for this risk factor. Must correspond to a period of significant financial stress relevant to the factor's asset class (e.g., 2008-09-01 for the Global Financial Crisis).
reference: BCBS FRTB — Stressed ES Calibration; Basel II.5 Stressed VaR Requirements; Fed DFAST Stress Scenarios

description: Granular sub-classification within the primary risk class (e.g., 'general_interest_rate_risk', 'specific_risk', 'counterparty_credit_risk', 'settlement_risk', 'concentration_risk'). Used for detailed regulatory reporting and internal risk appetite segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: general_interest_rate_risk|specific_risk|counterparty_credit_risk|settlement_risk|concentration_risk|basis_risk|vega_risk|curvature_risk — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS FRTB — Sensitivity-Based Method Sub-Risk Classes; EBA ITS on Market Risk

description: Maturity or tenor of the risk factor along the yield curve or term structure (e.g., '3M', '1Y', '10Y'). Applicable to interest rate, credit spread, and volatility surface factors. Null for spot FX rates and equity spot factors. Aligns with FRTB prescribed tenor vertices for delta sensitivity calculations.
reference: BCBS FRTB Article 325 — Prescribed Tenor Vertices; ISDA SIMM Tenor Buckets

description: Numeric representation of the risk factor tenor expressed in calendar days (e.g., 90 for 3M, 365 for 1Y). Enables precise interpolation between tenor vertices in risk model calculations and facilitates sorting and bucketing of term structure factors.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Interpolation Standards; ISDA Risk Factor Tenor Conventions

description: Reference to the historical time series record in the market data repository that stores the daily observations for this risk factor. Enables direct linkage from the factor definition to its underlying data for backtesting, calibration, and RFET assessment.
reference: BCBS 239 — Risk Data Aggregation and Reporting; BCBS FRTB Data Requirements

description: Unit in which the risk factor value is expressed (e.g., 'percent', 'basis_points', 'USD_per_barrel', 'index_points', 'USD_per_troy_oz'). Essential for correct interpretation of factor values, shock magnitudes, and sensitivity calculations across heterogeneous risk factor types.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Risk Factor Value Conventions; ISDA Risk Factor Taxonomy

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this risk factor record. Tracks changes to factor definitions, calibration parameters, or regulatory classifications. Essential for change management audit trails and regulatory examination readiness.
reference: BCBS 239 — Data Lineage and Audit Trail; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: Statistical confidence level used when computing Value at Risk (VaR) for this risk factor (e.g., 0.9900 for 99%, 0.9750 for 97.5% ES under FRTB). Stored as a decimal fraction. Governs the tail risk threshold for internal risk limits and regulatory capital calculations.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Expected Shortfall at 97.5% Confidence; Basel II.5 VaR at 99% Confidence; Fed CCAR Stress Testing

description: Percentage contribution of this risk factor to the total portfolio VaR as of the last calculation date. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0523 = 5.23%). Used for risk attribution reporting, limit monitoring, and identification of dominant risk drivers in the trading book.
reference: BCBS FRTB — Risk Attribution; Basel III Market Risk Capital; Internal Risk Appetite Framework

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each daily liquidity risk measurement record. Primary key for the liquidity_metric data product in the risk domain.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) LCR/NSFR Framework

description: Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: LCR/NSFR liquidity metrics are calculated and reported for specific accounting periods, aligned with financial close cycles and regulatory reporting calendars. Essential for period-based liquidity ris

Foreign key linking to risk.appetite. Business justification: Liquidity metrics (LCR, NSFR) are monitored against risk appetite statements that define the bank's tolerance for liquidity risk. The liquidity_metric table has regulatory minimums (lcr_regulatory_min

Foreign key linking to treasury.cash_flow_forecast. Business justification: Liquidity metrics (LCR, NSFR, survival horizon) are computed using cash flow forecasts as primary inputs. Linking liquidity_metric to the cash_flow_forecast that fed its calculation provides regulator
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the liquidity metric amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency liquidity reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; BCBS LCR Standard

description: Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: ESMA guidelines on liquidity stress testing for UCITS and AIFMD require fund-level liquidity metrics (LCR, survival horizon, redemption coverage ratio). liquidity_metric currently links to legal_entit

Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: LCR and NSFR are jurisdiction-specific regulatory ratios with different minimum thresholds (EU CRR, US LCR rule, Basel III). Regulatory liquidity reporting to local supervisors requires jurisdiction-l

Foreign key linking to treasury.liquidity_ratio. Business justification: Risk monitoring references treasury-calculated LCR/NSFR ratios for limit monitoring, breach detection, and regulatory reporting. Risk appetite framework requires tracking actual treasury-calculated ra

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: LCR and NSFR liquidity metrics are calculated specifically to demonstrate compliance with Basel III liquidity obligations. Compliance teams must link each metric calculation to the specific regulatory
Reference to the legal entity for which this liquidity metric record is calculated. Supports entity-level regulatory reporting to Fed, OCC, and PRA.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed); Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC); Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA)

description: Foreign key linking to risk.risk_limit. Business justification: Liquidity metrics (LCR, NSFR) are monitored against regulatory and internal risk limits. The liquidity_metric table has is_breach and breach_severity flags but no reference to the specific limit being

Foreign key linking to risk.stress_scenario. Business justification: Liquidity metrics are calculated under various stress scenarios (baseline, adverse, severely adverse). The liquidity_metric table has stress_scenario_code field which should be replaced by FK to stres

Foreign key linking to risk.stress_test_run. Business justification: liquidity_metric already links to stress_scenario (EXISTS) but not to the specific stress_test_run execution. LCR and NSFR metrics computed under stress conditions (stressed_lcr_ratio, stressed_nsfr_r

Status of the ALCO review process for this liquidity metric record. Tracks whether the metric has been reviewed, escalated for breach, or approved by the Asset-Liability Committee for regulatory submission.
reference: Asset-Liability Committee (ALCO) Governance Framework; Federal Reserve SR 10-6

description: The date and time when the liquidity metric record was formally approved by the ALCO or risk officer for regulatory submission or internal reporting. Supports governance and audit trail requirements.
reference: ALCO Governance Framework; Federal Reserve SR 10-6; SOX Section 302/906

description: Total Available Stable Funding (ASF) representing the portion of capital and liabilities expected to be reliable over a one-year horizon. Numerator of the NSFR ratio.
reference: BCBS NSFR Standard (October 2014); Federal Reserve NSFR Rule

description: Severity classification of a regulatory liquidity breach based on the magnitude of the shortfall relative to the minimum threshold. Drives escalation level and remediation response timeframe per the institution's liquidity contingency plan.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 10-6; BCBS Sound Principles for Liquidity Risk Management; PRA Liquidity Standards

description: The date and time when the liquidity metric calculation was executed in the risk management platform. Supports audit trail, reprocessing identification, and T+1 regulatory submission deadlines.
reference: SAS Risk Management; Moody's RiskAuthority; AxiomSL Regulatory Reporting Platform

description: Current workflow status of the liquidity metric record indicating whether the calculation is preliminary, final, restated, under review, or approved for regulatory submission.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed); Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC); AxiomSL / Wolters Kluwer OneSumX Regulatory Reporting Platform

description: Total value of assets eligible for central bank repo or discount window operations (e.g., Fed discount window, Bank of England repo). Represents the ultimate liquidity backstop available to the institution.
reference: Federal Reserve Discount Window Policy; Bank of England Sterling Monetary Framework; BCBS Sound Principles for Liquidity Risk Management

description: Free-text commentary provided by the risk officer or ALCO analyst explaining material movements, breaches, restatements, or methodology changes for this liquidity metric record. Supports regulatory examination and internal audit.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 10-6; BCBS Sound Principles for Liquidity Risk Management; SOX

description: Expected cash outflows from drawdowns on committed credit and liquidity facilities extended to customers and counterparties over the 30-day LCR stress horizon. Includes undrawn revolving credit facilities.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

description: Indicates whether the liquidity metric is calculated on a solo (single legal entity), consolidated (group-wide), or sub-consolidated basis. Determines the perimeter of assets and liabilities included in LCR/NSFR calculations.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; Federal Reserve LCR Rule; EBA LCR Delegated Act

description: The date and time when this liquidity metric record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX); BCBS Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation (BCBS 239)

description: Total value of assets pledged as collateral or otherwise encumbered (e.g., repo, covered bonds, central bank pledges) and therefore excluded from the HQLA buffer. Critical for accurate HQLA calculation.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; EBA Asset Encumbrance Guidelines (EBA/GL/2014/03)

description: Total expected gross cash inflows over the 30-day LCR stress horizon, capped at 75% of gross outflows per Basel III rules. Includes contractual loan repayments and securities maturities.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

description: Total expected gross cash outflows over the 30-day LCR stress horizon before netting of inflows. Includes retail deposit runoff, wholesale funding outflows, and off-balance-sheet commitments.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

description: Total stock of High Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA) after applicable haircuts, expressed in the reporting currency. Comprises Level 1, Level 2A, and Level 2B assets as defined under the Basel III LCR framework.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

business_glossary_term: High Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA) Level 1 Amount
description: Stock of Level 1 HQLA assets (e.g., central bank reserves, sovereign bonds with 0% risk weight) with no haircut applied. Highest quality component of the HQLA buffer.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

business_glossary_term: High Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA) Level 2A Amount
description: Stock of Level 2A HQLA assets (e.g., agency MBS, covered bonds, sovereign bonds with 20% risk weight) subject to a 15% haircut. Capped at 40% of total HQLA.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

business_glossary_term: High Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA) Level 2B Amount
description: Stock of Level 2B HQLA assets (e.g., non-agency RMBS, corporate bonds, equities) subject to haircuts of 25–50%. Capped at 15% of total HQLA.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

description: Total liquidity resources available intraday at the start of the business day, including central bank reserves, committed intraday credit facilities, and unencumbered liquid assets accessible within the day.
reference: BCBS Monitoring Tools for Intraday Liquidity Management (April 2013); Federal Reserve Intraday Credit Policy

description: Maximum intraday liquidity requirement observed during the reporting day, representing the peak net cumulative position across all payment systems (RTGS, ACH, SWIFT). Supports intraday liquidity monitoring per BCBS monitoring tools.
reference: BCBS Monitoring Tools for Intraday Liquidity Management (April 2013); Federal Reserve Intraday Credit Policy

description: Flag indicating whether the liquidity metric breaches the applicable regulatory minimum threshold (LCR < 100% or NSFR < 100%). Triggers escalation to ALCO and regulatory notification procedures.
reference: Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249); BCBS LCR Standard; PRA Liquidity Standards

description: Excess HQLA above the regulatory minimum LCR requirement, representing the institution's liquidity cushion. Calculated as HQLA minus (net cash outflows × regulatory minimum LCR). Used in ALCO reporting and internal liquidity stress testing.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; Federal Reserve LCR Rule; ALCO Reporting Standards

description: The Liquidity Coverage Ratio expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.25 = 125%). Calculated as HQLA divided by total net cash outflows over a 30-day stress period. Regulatory minimum is 100% (1.00) under Basel III / Fed LCR Rule.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249); OCC; PRA

description: The applicable regulatory minimum LCR threshold for this entity as of the metric date (e.g., 1.00 for 100%). May vary by jurisdiction (Fed, OCC, PRA) and entity classification (GSIB, non-GSIB).
reference: Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249); OCC; PRA; BCBS LCR Standard

description: Indicates whether there are legal, regulatory, or operational restrictions on the transfer of liquidity between entities within the group (e.g., ring-fencing, local regulatory requirements). Affects consolidated vs. solo LCR/NSFR calculations.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; PRA Ring-Fencing Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation W

description: The business date (as-of date) for which the liquidity metrics are calculated. Represents the daily snapshot date used for LCR, NSFR, and intraday liquidity reporting.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); BCBS NSFR Standard (October 2014)

description: Classification of the liquidity metric record indicating which regulatory or internal liquidity measure is captured. Values: LCR (Liquidity Coverage Ratio), NSFR (Net Stable Funding Ratio), INTRADAY (intraday liquidity position), SURVIVAL_HORIZON, STRESS (internal stress test metric).
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; BCBS NSFR Standard; Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

description: Total net cash outflows over the 30-calendar-day stress period, calculated as total expected cash outflows minus the minimum of total expected cash inflows and 75% of total expected cash outflows. Denominator of the LCR ratio.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard (January 2013); Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

description: The Net Stable Funding Ratio expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.10 = 110%). Calculated as Available Stable Funding (ASF) divided by Required Stable Funding (RSF). Regulatory minimum is 100% (1.00) under Basel III.
reference: BCBS NSFR Standard (October 2014); Federal Reserve NSFR Rule (12 CFR Part 249); EBA NSFR Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) Regulatory Minimum
description: The applicable regulatory minimum NSFR threshold for this entity as of the metric date (e.g., 1.00 for 100%). Supports compliance monitoring against Fed/OCC/EBA NSFR rules.
reference: Federal Reserve NSFR Rule (12 CFR Part 249); EBA NSFR Guidelines; BCBS NSFR Standard

description: Frequency at which this liquidity metric is calculated and reported. Daily for LCR/intraday; monthly or quarterly for NSFR and internal stress metrics. Drives scheduling in the regulatory reporting platform.
reference: Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249); BCBS NSFR Standard; AxiomSL Regulatory Reporting Platform

description: Total Required Stable Funding (RSF) representing the amount of stable funding required to support assets and off-balance-sheet exposures over a one-year horizon. Denominator of the NSFR ratio.
reference: BCBS NSFR Standard (October 2014); Federal Reserve NSFR Rule

description: Expected cash outflows from retail deposits (stable and less stable) over the 30-day LCR stress horizon, applying Basel III run-off rates (3–10% for stable, 10–15% for less stable deposits).
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249); FDIC

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this liquidity metric data was sourced (e.g., SAS_RISK, MOODYS_RA, AXIOMSL, ONESUMX). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAS Risk Management; Moody's RiskAuthority; AxiomSL; Wolters Kluwer OneSumX

description: The number of calendar days the institution can survive under a combined idiosyncratic and market-wide stress scenario without accessing wholesale funding markets. Key internal liquidity risk metric reported to ALCO.
reference: BCBS Sound Principles for Liquidity Risk Management (2008); Federal Reserve SR 10-6; PRA Liquidity Standards

description: Total value of assets that are free from legal, regulatory, or contractual restrictions and available to be monetized or pledged as collateral. Supports HQLA eligibility assessment.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; EBA Asset Encumbrance Guidelines; Federal Reserve LCR Rule

description: The date and time when this liquidity metric record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks restatements, corrections, and ALCO-driven adjustments to liquidity metric values.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX); BCBS Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation (BCBS 239)

description: Sequential version number of the liquidity metric record, incremented on each restatement or correction. Enables tracking of preliminary vs. final vs. restated submissions for regulatory audit purposes.
reference: BCBS Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation (BCBS 239); AxiomSL Regulatory Reporting Platform

description: Expected cash outflows from wholesale funding sources (e.g., interbank deposits, commercial paper, repo) over the 30-day LCR stress horizon. Key component of gross cash outflow calculation.
reference: BCBS LCR Standard; Federal Reserve LCR Rule (12 CFR Part 249)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each daily mark-to-market risk position record in the trading book or banking book. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: FRTB regulatory capital reporting and FINREP require market risk positions to be attributed to specific accounting periods for period-end capital adequacy disclosure. position_date is a plain column;
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the position's native denomination. Used for FX translation to reporting currency and FX risk sensitivity calculation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; BCBS FRTB d352 FX Risk

description: Foreign key linking to risk.risk_factor. Business justification: Each market risk position is exposed to specific risk factors (interest rate, FX, equity, credit spread). The market_risk_position table has risk_factor_code field which should be replaced by FK to ri

Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: Market risk positions held within fund portfolios must be aggregated at the fund level for UCITS/AIFMD VaR reporting and regulatory capital calculations. Risk systems need to attribute VaR, ES, and se
Reference to the financial instrument (security, derivative, loan, deposit) underlying this risk position. Links to the instrument master in the trading or risk system.
reference: Trading and Order Management System (Murex/Calypso); ISO 10962 CFI

description: Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Market risk positions must be attributed to legal entities for FRTB regulatory capital calculations, consolidated trading book reporting, and financial statement fair value disclosures. Essential for

Foreign key linking to collateral.margin_agreement. Business justification: FRTB-CVA capital charge calculation requires OTC derivative trading book positions to reference their governing ISDA/CSA margin agreement. The margin agreement's collateral terms directly reduce CVA c

Foreign key linking to collateral.netting_set. Business justification: SA-CCR and IMM regulatory capital calculations require each OTC derivative market risk position to be assigned to a netting set. Without this FK, the model cannot aggregate positions into netting sets
Reference to the counterparty or issuer associated with this risk position. Used for counterparty credit risk aggregation, CVA computation, and large exposure reporting.
reference: BCBS Basel III; CRR Article 390 Large Exposures

description: Foreign key linking to ledger.profit_center. Business justification: Trading book P&L attribution and RAROC reporting require market risk positions to reference the responsible profit center. This enables daily P&L attribution by desk/profit center — a core front-offic

Foreign key linking to reference.rate_benchmark. Business justification: Market risk positions in interest rate products (swaps, FRNs, bonds) require benchmark rate linkage for DV01/CS01 sensitivity calculation and FRTB risk factor assignment. IBOR transition tracking also

Foreign key linking to risk.risk_limit. Business justification: Market risk positions are monitored against VaR limits, position limits, and concentration limits. The market_risk_position table has var_1d_99, var_10d_99, and notional_amount but no reference to the
