Foreign key linking to risk.stress_scenario. Business justification: Market risk positions are stressed under defined scenarios for FRTB and IRRBB (Interest Rate Risk in Banking Book). The market_risk_position table has irrbb_scenario field which should be replaced by

Foreign key linking to risk.stress_test_run. Business justification: market_risk_position links to stress_scenario (EXISTS) but not to the specific stress_test_run execution. Stressed VaR, stressed CVA, and FRTB capital charges computed during a stress test run need to
Reference to the trading desk or business unit responsible for this position. Under FRTB, trading desks are the primary unit for IMA approval and P&L attribution testing.
reference: FRTB BCBS d352 — Trading Desk Definition

description: Broad asset class classification of the instrument (e.g., equity, interest_rate, credit, fx, commodity, securitisation). Drives risk factor assignment, sensitivity bucketing, and capital charge calculation under FRTB SA and IMA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equity|interest_rate|credit|fx|commodity|securitisation|other — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — Risk Factor Classification; ISDA taxonomy

description: Regulatory classification of the position as either trading book (subject to market risk capital under FRTB) or banking book (subject to IRRBB framework). Determines applicable capital treatment and risk measurement methodology.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — Trading/Banking Book Boundary; BCBS IRRBB d368

description: Timestamp when this risk position record was first captured in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Credit Spread sensitivity — the change in MTM value for a 1 basis point widening in the credit spread of the underlying issuer or reference entity. Key measure for credit risk management and FRTB CSR (Credit Spread Risk) capital charge.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — Credit Spread Risk (CSR); ISDA SIMM

description: Credit Valuation Adjustment representing the market value of counterparty default risk embedded in OTC derivative positions. Reflects the expected loss due to counterparty credit deterioration or default. Required under Basel III CVA framework.
reference: BCBS Basel III CVA Framework d325; IFRS 13; CRR2 Article 381

description: First-order sensitivity of the position's MTM value to a unit change in the underlying risk factor price (e.g., equity price, FX spot rate, commodity price). Used for delta hedging and FRTB SA delta risk charge calculation.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — Delta Risk; ISDA SIMM Methodology

description: Dollar Value of a 01 (one basis point) shift in interest rates — the change in MTM value for a 1 basis point (0.01%) parallel upward shift in the yield curve. Primary interest rate sensitivity measure used for hedging and risk limit monitoring.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — Delta Sensitivity; ALCO Risk Reporting Standard

description: Sensitivity of the Economic Value of Equity to standardised interest rate shock scenarios under IRRBB. Measures the long-term impact of rate changes on the present value of banking book assets and liabilities. Used in IRRBB supervisory outlier test.
reference: BCBS IRRBB d368 — EVE; EBA IRRBB Guidelines; PRA SS31/15

description: Expected Shortfall (Conditional VaR) at 97.5% confidence level as required under FRTB IMA. Measures the average loss in the worst 2.5% of scenarios, replacing VaR as the primary IMA risk measure under FRTB.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — IMA Expected Shortfall; FRTB Article 325bb

business_glossary_term: Fundamental Review of the Trading Book (FRTB) Approach
description: Indicates whether the FRTB capital charge for this position is computed under the Internal Models Approach (IMA), Standardised Approach (SA), or IMA with SA fallback for non-modellable risk factors (NMRFs).
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — IMA vs SA Boundary

business_glossary_term: Fundamental Review of the Trading Book (FRTB) Capital Charge
description: Total FRTB market risk capital charge for this position, representing the higher of the IMA (Internal Models Approach) or SA (Standardised Approach) capital requirement as applicable. Reported to regulators under FRTB framework.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352; CRR2 Article 325; Fed FRTB NPR

description: Second-order (curvature) sensitivity of the position's MTM value to changes in the underlying risk factor. Captures non-linear risk for options and structured products. Used in FRTB SA curvature risk charge calculation.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — Curvature Risk; ISDA SIMM

description: Accounting hedge designation for the position under IFRS 9 or ASC 815. Determines whether hedge accounting is applied and the treatment of fair value changes in OCI vs P&L. Relevant for banking book hedges of interest rate and FX risk.
reference: IFRS 9 Chapter 6 — Hedge Accounting; ASC 815; BCBS IRRBB d368

description: Hypothetical P&L computed by revaluing the prior day's portfolio using current day's market prices, holding positions constant. Used in VaR backtesting and FRTB IMA P&L attribution test to validate risk model accuracy.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — P&L Attribution Test (PLAT); Basel 2.5 Backtesting

description: Specific instrument type classification (e.g., bond, equity, interest_rate_swap, fx_forward, credit_default_swap, option, futures, mortgage_backed_security). Used for product-level risk aggregation and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bond|equity|interest_rate_swap|fx_forward|credit_default_swap|option|futures|mortgage_backed_security|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 10962 CFI; ISDA Product Taxonomy; FRTB BCBS d352

description: Incremental Risk Charge capturing default and migration risk for credit-sensitive positions in the trading book over a one-year capital horizon at 99.9% confidence. Applicable to non-securitisation credit positions under Basel 2.5.
reference: BCBS Basel 2.5 — Incremental Risk Charge; CRR Article 372

description: Indicates whether the primary risk factor for this position passes the FRTB Risk Factor Eligibility Test (RFET) and is classified as modellable. Non-modellable risk factors (NMRFs) attract a separate stress scenario capital add-on under FRTB IMA.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — Risk Factor Eligibility Test (RFET)

description: The contractual maturity or expiry date of the instrument underlying this position. Used for repricing gap bucketing, duration calculation, and IRRBB cash flow mapping.
reference: BCBS IRRBB d368; FRTB BCBS d352; IFRS 9

description: Current mark-to-market fair value of the position in the reporting currency as of the position date. Represents the present value of future cash flows discounted at current market rates. Core input for P&L attribution and capital calculations.
reference: IFRS 13 Fair Value Measurement; BCBS FRTB d352; ASC 820

description: Sensitivity of Net Interest Income to a standardised interest rate shock scenario (e.g., +200bps parallel shift). Applicable to banking book positions under IRRBB framework. Measures short-term earnings impact of rate changes.
reference: BCBS IRRBB d368; EBA IRRBB Guidelines EBA/GL/2018/02

description: The face value or contractual notional amount of the position in the instrument's native currency. For derivatives, this is the reference principal used to compute cash flows and risk sensitivities.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352; ISDA Master Agreement

description: Daily P&L for the position representing the change in MTM value from the prior business day. Used for P&L attribution analysis, backtesting of VaR models, and FRTB IMA P&L attribution test (PLAT) compliance.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — P&L Attribution Test; IFRS 9

description: The business date (valuation date) for which this risk position snapshot is captured. Represents the as-of date for all MTM values, sensitivities, and risk measures on this record.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352; IRRBB BCBS d368

description: Externally-known business identifier for the risk position as assigned by the source trading or risk system (e.g., Murex position ID or Calypso deal reference). Used for reconciliation with the system of record.
Current lifecycle state of the risk position indicating whether it is actively open, closed out, suspended pending review, awaiting settlement, or cancelled.
reference: FRTB (Fundamental Review of the Trading Book) — BCBS d352

description: ISO 4217 three-letter code for the base reporting currency (typically USD or EUR) into which all MTM values and risk measures are translated for regulatory and management reporting.

Time bucket classification for the repricing gap analysis (e.g., overnight, 1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, 2Y, 5Y, 10Y+). Indicates when the position's interest rate resets or matures, driving IRRBB gap analysis and NII sensitivity computation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: overnight|1M|3M|6M|1Y|2Y|5Y|10Y_plus — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS IRRBB d368; EBA IRRBB Guidelines EBA/GL/2018/02

description: The net difference between rate-sensitive assets and rate-sensitive liabilities repricing within a given time bucket. Used in IRRBB repricing gap analysis to identify mismatches that create NII at risk. Applicable to banking book positions.
reference: BCBS IRRBB d368 — Repricing Gap Analysis; ALCO Reporting Standard

description: Risk-Weighted Assets attributable to market risk for this position, computed as the capital charge multiplied by 12.5 (reciprocal of the 8% minimum capital ratio). Used in CET1 ratio and CCAR/DFAST capital adequacy reporting.
reference: BCBS Basel III; CRR Article 92; CCAR/DFAST Fed Guidance

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this risk position was sourced (e.g., MUREX for trading book positions, SAS_RISK for risk aggregations, AXIOMSL for regulatory submissions). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; SOX Section 404

description: Stressed VaR computed using a continuous 12-month historical stress period calibrated to significant financial stress (e.g., 2008 financial crisis). Required under Basel 2.5 and used as a multiplier component in market risk capital calculation.
reference: BCBS Basel 2.5 — Stressed VaR; CRR Article 365

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this risk position record, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.

Ten-day Value at Risk at 99% confidence level, scaled from the 1-day VaR using the square-root-of-time rule or direct simulation. Used for regulatory capital calculation under Basel 2.5 market risk framework.
reference: BCBS Basel 2.5 Market Risk Amendment; CRR Article 365

description: One-day Value at Risk at 99% confidence level computed using historical simulation methodology. Represents the maximum expected loss over a one-day horizon with 99% probability. Primary regulatory VaR metric under Basel 2.5 and FRTB IMA.
reference: BCBS Basel 2.5; FRTB BCBS d352 IMA; Fed SR 11-7

description: Sensitivity of the position's MTM value to a 1% change in implied volatility of the underlying. Applicable to options and volatility-sensitive instruments. Used in FRTB SA vega risk charge calculation.
reference: BCBS FRTB d352 — Vega Risk; ISDA SIMM Methodology

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each risk appetite statement or threshold record within the enterprise risk appetite framework.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Risk Appetite Framework; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7

description: Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Risk appetite statements are set per regulatory jurisdiction — DFAST appetite differs from PRA/EBA requirements. Board-approved appetite documents reference specific regulatory frameworks tied to juri

Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Board-approved risk appetite frameworks are entity-specific; SREP and ICAAP regulatory submissions require appetite statements attributed to specific legal entities. The plain column legal_entity on a

Reference to the risk_appetite_id of the newer version that supersedes this record when approval_status is 'superseded'. Enables forward-chaining of the version history for audit and regulatory review purposes.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; BCBS Corporate Governance; FSB Risk Appetite Framework

description: Qualitative board-approved appetite level indicating the degree of risk the institution is willing to accept in pursuit of its strategic objectives. Ranges from low (minimal risk tolerance) to high (aggressive risk-taking within regulatory bounds).
reference: FSB Principles for Sound Risk Appetite Frameworks; BCBS Corporate Governance Principles

description: Date on which the governing body formally approved this risk appetite statement. Critical for audit trail, SOX compliance, and regulatory examination documentation.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; BCBS Corporate Governance Principles; Fed SR 12-17

description: Current lifecycle status of the risk appetite statement within the governance approval workflow. Tracks progression from initial drafting through board approval to eventual supersession or retirement.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; BCBS Corporate Governance Principles; SOX Section 302

description: Defines the escalation tier that must be notified upon breach of this appetite threshold. Establishes the governance response chain from management-level notification through CRO to Board Risk Committee escalation.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; BCBS Corporate Governance Principles; Fed SR 11-7

description: Maximum number of calendar days within which a formal remediation plan must be submitted to the designated escalation body following a breach of this appetite threshold.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; OCC Risk Governance Handbook; Fed SR 11-7

description: Timestamp when this risk appetite record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX, BCBS 239 data lineage standards, and regulatory examination documentation.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; BCBS 239 Data Quality Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether the appetite threshold represents a minimum floor (e.g., CET1 ratio must be at least X%), a maximum ceiling (e.g., NPL ratio must not exceed Y%), a target value, or a permissible range.
reference: BCBS Basel III; Fed CCAR; IFRS 9

description: Date from which this risk appetite statement becomes binding and operative across the institution. Aligns with the board approval cycle, typically annual or triggered by material risk profile changes.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; BCBS Supervisory Review Process; Fed CCAR Annual Cycle

description: Date on which this risk appetite statement ceases to be operative and must be reviewed, renewed, or superseded. Null for open-ended statements that remain in force until explicitly retired.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; BCBS Annual Review Requirements

description: The governing body that approved this risk appetite statement (e.g., Board of Directors, Board Risk Committee, ALCO, Executive Risk Committee). Establishes the authority level and escalation chain for breach management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: board|board_risk_committee|alco|executive_risk_committee|credit_committee|audit_committee|cro_office — promote to reference product]
reference: FSB Corporate Governance Principles; BCBS Corporate Governance Guidelines; Fed SR 12-17

description: Indicates whether this risk appetite statement has received formal Board of Directors or Board Risk Committee approval (True) or is still in draft/management-approved status (False). Required for regulatory examination and SOX attestation.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; BCBS Corporate Governance Principles; Fed SR 12-17

description: Indicates whether this risk appetite statement is expressed as a quantitative metric with a numeric threshold (True) or as a qualitative statement without a numeric bound (False). Qualitative statements include zero-tolerance policies for regulatory violations or reputational risk.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; BCBS Risk Appetite Guidance

description: Indicates whether this appetite threshold is set at or derived from a regulatory minimum requirement (True) or represents an internally-set management buffer above the regulatory floor (False). Critical for distinguishing regulatory compliance floors from internal risk management buffers.
reference: BCBS Basel III Minimum Capital Requirements; Fed CCAR Minimum Ratios; OCC Capital Adequacy Standards

description: Reference code linking this risk appetite statement to the corresponding Key Risk Indicator (KRI) in the enterprise GRC system. Enables automated monitoring of appetite utilization against real-time KRI readings from SAS Risk Management / Moodys RiskAuthority.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk SMA; OCC KRI Guidance; Fed SR 11-7

description: Date on which this risk appetite statement was most recently reviewed and reaffirmed or amended by the governing body. Provides audit evidence of ongoing governance oversight.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; SOX Section 302; BCBS Corporate Governance

description: Hard operational limit assigned to a specific business line, desk, or portfolio that must not be exceeded. Derived from and subordinate to the enterprise-level appetite threshold. Used in daily risk limit monitoring within the Trading and Order Management System (Murex/Calypso).
reference: BCBS FRTB; FINRA Rule 4370; Fed SR 11-7; ISDA CSA

description: The specific Line of Business (LOB) to which this appetite statement applies (e.g., 'Commercial Lending', 'Investment Banking', 'Retail Banking', 'Securities Trading', 'Wealth Management'). Null if the statement applies enterprise-wide.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk SMA; Fed CCAR LOB Segmentation; FINRA LOB Reporting

description: Formal definition of how the risk metric is calculated or assessed, including the numerator, denominator, observation period, and data sources. Ensures consistent measurement across business lines and regulatory submissions.
reference: BCBS Basel III; IFRS 9; FASB CECL; Fed SR 11-7

description: Name of the quantitative or qualitative metric used to measure risk against this appetite statement (e.g., 'CET1 Ratio', 'VaR 99% 10-day', 'LCR', 'NSFR', 'NPL Ratio', 'ECL Coverage Ratio').
reference: BCBS Basel III; Fed CCAR/DFAST; IFRS 9; FASB CECL

description: Unit in which the risk metric is expressed (e.g., percentage for CET1 ratio, currency_usd for VaR dollar amount, basis_points for credit spread limits, days for LCR horizon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage|ratio|currency_usd|currency_local|basis_points|count|score|days — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS Basel III; ISDA Definitions; FASB ASC 820

description: Scheduled date for the next formal review of this risk appetite statement by the designated governance body. Derived from the last review date and review frequency; used to drive governance workflow alerts.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; BCBS Annual Review Requirements

description: Full text of the board-approved qualitative risk appetite statement for non-quantifiable risk types (e.g., 'The Bank has zero tolerance for breaches of AML/BSA regulations' or 'The Bank will not engage in activities that materially damage its reputation'). Populated when is_quantitative is False.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; FinCEN BSA/AML Requirements; BCBS Corporate Governance

description: Lower boundary of the acceptable risk range when direction is 'range'. Applicable for metrics such as NIM targets or LCR operating ranges where both a floor and ceiling are defined.
reference: BCBS LCR/NSFR Standards; ALCO Policy Framework

description: Upper boundary of the acceptable risk range when direction is 'range'. Used alongside range_lower_bound to define the full operating corridor for metrics managed within a band.
reference: BCBS LCR/NSFR Standards; ALCO Policy Framework

business_glossary_term: Risk-Adjusted Return on Capital (RAROC) Hurdle Rate
description: Minimum Risk-Adjusted Return on Capital (RAROC) threshold that business activities must meet to be consistent with this risk appetite statement. Used in capital allocation decisions and new product approval processes.
reference: BCBS Pillar 2 ICAAP; Fed CCAR Capital Planning; RAROC Methodology

description: Mandated frequency at which this risk appetite statement must be formally reviewed and reaffirmed by the governing body. Drives the governance calendar for ALCO and Board Risk Committee scheduling.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; BCBS Supervisory Review; OCC Risk Governance Handbook

description: Primary risk category to which this appetite statement applies. Aligns with the enterprise risk taxonomy: credit, market, liquidity, operational, reputational, or strategic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit|market|liquidity|operational|reputational|strategic|compliance|model|concentration — promote to reference product if taxonomy expands]
reference: BCBS Basel III Framework; OCC Risk Governance Handbook; FSB Risk Appetite Framework

description: Granular sub-classification within the primary risk category (e.g., 'Counterparty Credit Risk', 'Interest Rate Risk in the Banking Book (IRRBB)', 'Intraday Liquidity Risk', 'Cyber Operational Risk'). Supports drill-down analytics in CCAR and DFAST reporting.
reference: BCBS IRRBB Standards; FRTB Framework; OCC Operational Risk Guidance

description: Maximum Risk-Weighted Assets (RWA) allocated to the applicable business line or portfolio under this appetite statement. Expressed in the institution's reporting currency. Supports capital adequacy management under Basel III Standardized Approach (SA) and Internal Ratings-Based (IRB) Approach.
reference: BCBS Basel III RWA Framework; Fed CCAR; OCC Capital Adequacy Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this risk appetite record originates or is maintained (e.g., SAS_RM for SAS Risk Management, MOODYS_RA for Moodys RiskAuthority, AXIOMSL for AxiomSL regulatory reporting platform, MANUAL for board-documented statements).
reference: Enterprise Data Governance Policy; BCBS Data Quality Principles (BCBS 239)

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the risk appetite statement, used in board documentation, ALCO packs, and regulatory submissions (e.g., RA-CREDIT-2024-001).
reference: BCBS Risk Appetite Framework; Federal Reserve CCAR Guidance

description: Human-readable name of the risk appetite statement as approved by the Board Risk Committee (e.g., 'Maximum Credit Risk Concentration — Single Obligor').
reference: BCBS Risk Appetite Framework; FSB Principles for Sound Risk Appetite Frameworks

description: Indicates which stress testing scenario(s) this appetite threshold applies to: baseline (normal conditions), adverse (moderate stress), severely adverse (severe stress), or all scenarios. Directly maps to CCAR/DFAST scenario definitions.
reference: Fed CCAR/DFAST Stress Testing Guidance; BCBS Stress Testing Principles

description: Board-approved quantitative value representing the maximum level of risk the institution is willing to accept under normal operating conditions. For example, a CET1 ratio floor of 11.5% or a VaR limit of USD 50 million.
reference: BCBS Basel III CET1 Requirements; Fed CCAR Minimum Capital Ratios; IFRS 9 ECL

description: Maximum acceptable deviation from the risk appetite threshold before mandatory escalation is triggered. Represents the outer boundary of acceptable risk exposure (e.g., CET1 floor of 10.5% as the tolerance boundary below the 11.5% appetite).
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; BCBS Supervisory Review Process (Pillar 2); OCC Risk Governance

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this risk appetite record in the data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance per BCBS 239 data governance standards.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; BCBS 239 Data Quality Principles; GDPR Article 5

description: Sequential version number of this risk appetite statement, incremented each time the statement is formally amended and re-approved. Enables version history tracking and audit trail for regulatory examination.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; BCBS Corporate Governance Principles; Fed SR 12-17

description: Early-warning trigger level set between the appetite threshold and tolerance threshold. Breaching this level initiates management-level monitoring and pre-emptive action before the tolerance boundary is reached. Supports KRI monitoring frameworks.
reference: FSB Risk Appetite Framework; Fed SR 11-7; OCC KRI Guidance

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the risk assessment record. Primary key for the risk_assessment data product in the enterprise risk management lakehouse.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) — Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk

description: Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite. Business justification: Risk assessments evaluate residual risk against the enterprise risk appetite framework. The risk_assessment table has residual_risk_rating and residual_risk_score — these are compared to risk_appetite

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: RCSA (Risk and Control Self-Assessment) is performed at the channel level in banking. Regulators and internal audit require channel-scoped risk assessments (e.g., digital channel cyber risk, branch op

Foreign key linking to asset.fund. Business justification: RCSA (Risk and Control Self-Assessment) processes in asset management are conducted at the fund level. Assessments covering fund-specific operational controls, compliance risks, and investment process

Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: RCSA (Risk and Control Self-Assessments) are conducted per regulatory jurisdiction to comply with local supervisory requirements (SR 11-7 in US, SS1/23 in UK). Linking assessment to jurisdiction enabl

Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: SOX compliance and RCSA governance require risk assessments to be attributed to specific legal entities for regulatory reporting (SREP, ICAAP). legal_entity_code on assessment is a denormalized repres

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Risk and Control Self-Assessments (RCSAs) are scoped to specific regulatory obligations (e.g., Basel III, Dodd-Frank). Banks must demonstrate that RCSA findings map to compliance obligations for regul

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_exam. Business justification: RCSAs are frequently initiated in response to regulatory exam findings (MRAs/MRAIAs). Examiners track whether prior exam findings triggered new assessments. Banks must link assessments to the exam tha

Documented plan of remediation actions to address identified control gaps or elevated risk ratings. Includes action steps, responsible parties, and target completion dates. Tracked in the GRC platform for management information reporting.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; COSO ERM Framework; OCC Matters Requiring Attention (MRA) Guidance

description: Date on which the risk assessment was formally approved by the designated approver. Marks the transition from 'under_review' to 'approved' status and triggers downstream reporting and action plan activation.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; SOX Section 404; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment

description: Externally-known business identifier for the assessment, used in GRC platform references, board risk committee reports, and regulatory submissions. Format: RA-{CATEGORY}-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; OCC Supervisory Guidance on Operational Risk

description: The principal business event date on which the risk assessment was conducted or formally initiated. Used as the reference date for inherent risk and control effectiveness ratings.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; IFRS 9 — Impairment; CCAR/DFAST Supervisory Guidance

description: Current lifecycle state of the risk assessment record within the GRC workflow. Drives escalation, approval routing, and reporting eligibility.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Discriminator identifying the methodology and scope of the assessment. 'enterprise_risk_assessment' covers holistic enterprise-wide evaluations; 'rcsa' denotes Risk and Control Self-Assessment; 'thematic_review' covers targeted deep-dives; 'icaap' aligns with Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process; 'orsa' aligns with Own Risk and Solvency Assessment.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Handbook — Internal Controls

description: Narrative description of the key control(s) in place to mitigate the identified risk. Captures control design, automation level, and frequency of operation. Used in RCSA documentation and internal audit evidence packages.
reference: COSO Internal Control — Integrated Framework; OCC Handbook — Internal Controls; SOX Section 404

description: Assessment of how effectively the existing controls mitigate the identified inherent risk. Derived from control design adequacy and operating effectiveness testing. Drives residual risk calculation and remediation prioritization.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; COSO Internal Control — Integrated Framework; OCC Handbook — Internal Controls

description: Classification of the primary control mechanism associated with the assessed risk. 'preventive' controls stop risk events from occurring; 'detective' controls identify events after occurrence; 'corrective' controls remediate impact; 'directive' controls establish required behaviors.
reference: COSO Internal Control — Integrated Framework; OCC Handbook — Internal Controls; BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk

description: Timestamp when the risk assessment record was first captured in the GRC platform or risk management system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; BCBS BCBS239 — Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation

description: Narrative summary of key findings identified during the risk assessment, including control gaps, risk drivers, and significant observations. Used in board risk committee packs, ICAAP documentation, and regulatory examination responses.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Qualitative assessment of the severity of financial, operational, reputational, or regulatory impact if the risk event materializes. Combined with likelihood to derive the inherent risk score.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; COSO ERM Framework; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment

description: Risk rating reflecting the gross level of risk before considering the effect of controls. Assessed based on likelihood and impact of the risk materializing in the absence of mitigating controls. Core input to the risk appetite framework and ICAAP capital assessment.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment

description: Flag indicating whether the assessed risk has been classified as material under the enterprise risk appetite framework or ICAAP materiality assessment. Material risks receive enhanced monitoring, board-level reporting, and dedicated capital allocation consideration.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; EBA ICAAP Guidelines

description: Flag indicating whether this risk assessment was initiated or escalated as a result of a regulatory examination finding, Matters Requiring Attention (MRA), or supervisory letter. Drives priority handling and regulatory response tracking.
reference: OCC Matters Requiring Attention (MRA) Guidance; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; FDIC Examination Guidance

description: Code referencing the Key Risk Indicator (KRI) associated with this risk assessment. Links the assessment to the quantitative KRI monitoring framework for threshold breach tracking and early warning signal generation.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; Institute of Operational Risk — KRI Sound Practice Guidance

description: Date on which the most recent formal test of the associated control was completed. Used to assess control currency, identify overdue testing, and support internal audit scheduling.
reference: SOX Section 404; COSO Internal Control — Integrated Framework; OCC Handbook — Internal Controls

description: Qualitative assessment of the probability that the identified risk event will materialize within the assessment horizon. Combined with impact rating to derive the inherent risk score on the risk heat map.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; COSO ERM Framework; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment

description: The business line or division to which the assessed risk is attributed (e.g., 'retail_banking', 'commercial_lending', 'investment_banking', 'wealth_management', 'treasury'). Used for LOB-level risk aggregation and CCAR/DFAST capital allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail_banking|commercial_lending|investment_banking|wealth_management|treasury|asset_management|payments|corporate_trust — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS Basel III — Business Line Mapping; Federal Reserve CCAR Supervisory Guidance

description: Methodology applied to conduct the risk assessment. 'qualitative' relies on expert judgment; 'quantitative' uses statistical models (e.g., VaR, ECL); 'hybrid' combines both; 'scenario_analysis' applies stress scenarios; 'loss_data_analysis' uses historical loss event data.
reference: BCBS Basel III — IRB Approach; BCBS SMA Framework; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic review or reassessment of this risk assessment record. Ensures timely refresh of risk ratings and control effectiveness in line with the risk appetite framework review cycle.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment

description: Name of the business process or activity being assessed, primarily used in RCSA assessments (e.g., 'Loan Origination', 'Trade Settlement', 'AML Transaction Monitoring'). Provides process-level granularity for operational risk management.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; OCC Handbook — Internal Controls

description: Target date by which all remediation actions in the action plan must be completed. Used for tracking overdue items, escalation triggers, and regulatory commitment management.
reference: OCC Matters Requiring Attention (MRA) Guidance; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk

description: Current status of the remediation action plan associated with this risk assessment. Drives escalation workflows, management reporting, and regulatory commitment tracking in the GRC platform.
reference: OCC Matters Requiring Attention (MRA) Guidance; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk

description: Net risk rating after accounting for the effectiveness of existing controls. Represents the remaining risk exposure and is the primary metric for risk appetite comparison and escalation decisions.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; COSO ERM Framework

description: Quantitative residual risk score (typically on a 1–25 or 1–100 scale) derived from the product of likelihood and impact ratings after control adjustment. Used for risk heat map positioning and quantitative risk aggregation in the GRC platform.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; SAS Risk Management — RCSA Module

description: Primary risk taxonomy classification for the assessment. Aligns with the enterprise risk appetite framework and Basel III/IV risk category definitions. Drives routing to the appropriate risk owner and regulatory capital treatment.
reference: BCBS Basel III Framework; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment

description: Basel II/III operational risk event type classification applicable primarily to RCSA assessments. Maps to the seven Basel operational risk event categories used in Standardized Measurement Approach (SMA) capital calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_fraud|external_fraud|employment_practices|clients_products_business|damage_physical_assets|business_disruption|execution_delivery — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS Basel II — Operational Risk Event Type Classification; BCBS SMA Framework

description: Full name of the designated risk owner accountable for the assessed risk. Retained as a denormalized field for reporting and audit trail purposes independent of HR system changes.
reference: BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Three Lines of Defence Model

description: Granular risk classification within the primary risk category (e.g., 'fraud_risk', 'model_risk', 'settlement_risk', 'concentration_risk', 'interest_rate_risk_in_banking_book'). Supports detailed risk taxonomy reporting and KRI alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fraud_risk|model_risk|settlement_risk|concentration_risk|irrbb|cyber_risk|third_party_risk|conduct_risk — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS Basel III Framework; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this risk assessment record originated (e.g., 'SAS_RISK_MGMT', 'MOODYS_RISK_AUTH', 'AXIOMSL', 'MANUAL_GRC'). Supports data lineage, ETL reconciliation, and BCBS239 data quality reporting.
reference: BCBS239 — Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting; SOX Section 404

description: Frequency at which the effectiveness of the identified control is formally tested. Drives the control testing schedule in the GRC platform and informs the assurance calendar for internal audit and compliance functions.
reference: COSO Internal Control — Integrated Framework; SOX Section 404; OCC Handbook — Internal Controls

description: Identifies which line of defence conducted or is responsible for this risk assessment. 'first_line' is the business unit; 'second_line' is risk management or compliance; 'third_line' is internal audit. Supports governance framework reporting and assurance mapping.
reference: IIA Three Lines Model; BCBS Principles for the Sound Management of Operational Risk; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the risk assessment record. Used for change tracking, version control, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; BCBS239 — Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation

description: Sequential version number of the risk assessment record, incremented each time the assessment is formally revised and re-approved. Supports version history tracking, audit trail, and supersession management in the GRC platform.
reference: SOX Section 404; BCBS239 — Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation; OCC Handbook — Risk Assessment

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each KRI measurement record in the enterprise risk monitoring registry. Primary key for the kri_measurement data product.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Principles for Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting (BCBS 239)

description: Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Board risk reporting and regulatory KRI submissions (SREP, ICAAP) align KRI measurements to financial accounting periods for period-over-period trend analysis. measurement_period_start/end are plain d

Reference to the risk appetite statement from which the KRI thresholds (green/amber/red) were derived. Links this measurement to the governing risk appetite framework entry for threshold lineage and governance audit.
reference: Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239

description: Foreign key linking to risk.assessment. Business justification: KRI measurements are directly linked to risk assessments (RCSA). Both tables share kri_code as a string reference, indicating an intended relationship. Adding assessment_id to kri_measurement normaliz

Foreign key linking to channel.branch. Business justification: Branch-level KRI scorecards are a standard banking practice — regulators and internal audit track KRIs (fraud rate, complaint volume, cash discrepancy) at individual branch level. Existing channel_id

Foreign key linking to treasury.capital_ratio. Business justification: Capital ratio KRIs (CET1 ratio, leverage ratio, total capital ratio) are measured directly against capital_ratio records. Linking kri_measurement to capital_ratio enables direct traceability from KRI

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Key Risk Indicators are measured per channel (ATM downtime %, digital fraud rate, branch error rate). Channel-level KRI monitoring is standard in banking risk management for operational risk oversight

Foreign key linking to asset.fund_performance. Business justification: Investment risk KRIs (tracking error breach, maximum drawdown, Sharpe ratio threshold) are measured directly from fund performance records. Risk teams configure KRIs that trigger when fund_performance

Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: ICAAP and SREP regulatory submissions require KRI measurements to be attributed to specific legal entities. The plain column legal_entity on kri_measurement is a denormalized string; replacing with a

Foreign key linking to treasury.liquidity_position. Business justification: KRIs monitor specific liquidity position metrics for early warning indicators in risk appetite framework. Liquidity KRIs track intraday positions, tenor gaps, and scenario-based positions for breach d

Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: KRIs are defined to measure adherence to specific regulatory obligations (e.g., LCR KRI tied to Basel III liquidity obligation, capital ratio KRI tied to CRR capital obligation). Compliance teams use

Foreign key linking to risk.risk_limit. Business justification: KRI measurements track against defined thresholds and limits. The kri_measurement table has green/amber/red thresholds and breach_status — these are operationally linked to risk_limit definitions for

Foreign key linking to risk.stress_scenario. Business justification: kri_measurement has stress_scenario_flag (BOOLEAN) and stress_scenario_name (STRING) as denormalized references to a stress scenario. Adding stress_scenario_id normalizes this relationship, allowing K

The observed or calculated numeric value of the KRI for the measurement period. Represents the raw measurement result before threshold comparison (e.g., NPL ratio of 0.0325, LCR of 1.42, VaR of 15000000). Unit of measure is defined in the unit_of_measure field.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance

description: The boundary defining the warning (amber) zone for the KRI. Values breaching this threshold trigger heightened monitoring and management attention but do not yet require board-level escalation. Derived from the risk appetite framework.
reference: Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239

description: Workflow status of the KRI measurement record through the review and approval process. 'draft' indicates initial capture, 'submitted' indicates pending review, 'approved' indicates sign-off by risk governance, 'rejected' indicates the measurement requires correction and resubmission.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 302/404

description: The date and time at which the KRI measurement record was formally approved by the risk governance officer. Provides the audit timestamp for SOX and regulatory compliance purposes.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 302/404; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7

description: Name or employee identifier of the risk governance officer who approved this KRI measurement record. Required for SOX and regulatory audit trail. Classified confidential as it identifies internal personnel.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 302/404; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239

description: The RAG (Red-Amber-Green) status of the KRI measurement relative to defined thresholds. 'green' indicates within appetite, 'amber' indicates warning zone, 'red' indicates limit breach. Drives escalation workflows and board risk committee reporting.
reference: Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Federal Reserve System (Fed) CCAR Guidance

description: Description of the methodology or formula used to calculate the KRI value (e.g., 'HQLA / Net Cash Outflows over 30 days', 'NPL balance / Total Gross Loans', '99th percentile 10-day VaR using historical simulation'). Supports model governance and audit requirements.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7 Model Risk Management

description: Free-text narrative commentary provided by the KRI owner explaining the measurement result, contextualising any threshold breaches, or noting exceptional circumstances affecting the value (e.g., market dislocation, one-off events, methodology changes).
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239

description: The date and time at which this KRI measurement record was first created in the system. Provides the audit creation timestamp for data lineage, BCBS 239 compliance, and silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX)

description: Categorical data quality assessment for this KRI measurement record. 'passed' indicates data meets quality standards, 'warning' indicates minor data issues that do not invalidate the measurement, 'failed' indicates the measurement should not be used, 'under_review' indicates quality assessment is pending.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7

description: A numeric score (0.00 to 100.00) representing the assessed quality of the underlying data used to compute this KRI measurement. Supports BCBS 239 data quality attestation requirements and flags measurements with low data confidence for review.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239 Principles for Effective Risk Data Aggregation

description: The operational system of record from which the KRI measurement data was sourced (e.g., 'SAS Risk Management', 'Moodys RiskAuthority', 'Murex', 'Core Banking T24', 'AxiomSL'). Critical for data lineage and BCBS 239 data quality attestation.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7

description: The governance body or individual to whom the KRI breach has been escalated (e.g., 'ALCO', 'Board Risk Committee', 'CRO', 'Risk Management Committee'). Defines the escalation path for each breach severity level.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Corporate Governance Principles for Banks

description: The date on which the KRI breach was formally escalated to the designated escalation authority. Null if no escalation was required. Used to measure escalation timeliness against SLA targets.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance

description: Current status of the escalation workflow triggered by a KRI breach. 'not_required' for green-status KRIs, 'pending' when escalation has been initiated but not yet actioned, 'in_progress' when remediation is underway, 'resolved' when breach is remediated, 'overdue' when SLA has been breached.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance; Governance Risk and Compliance (GRC) Framework

description: The upper or lower boundary defining the acceptable (green) zone for the KRI. Values within this threshold indicate normal operating conditions and no escalation is required. Derived from the risk appetite framework.
reference: Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239

description: Indicates whether this KRI is included in board risk committee reporting packs (True) or is management-level only (False). Board-reported KRIs require higher data quality standards and formal sign-off per governance policy.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Corporate Governance Principles for Banks; Financial Stability Board (FSB)

description: Indicates whether this KRI is mandated by a regulatory body (True) or is an internally-defined management KRI (False). Regulatory KRIs (e.g., LCR, CET1, NSFR) require formal regulatory reporting and have prescribed minimum thresholds.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Basel III; Federal Reserve System (Fed) CCAR/DFAST; Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC)

description: Unique business identifier for the KRI definition in the enterprise KRI taxonomy. Follows the format KRI-<CATEGORY>-<SEQUENCE> (e.g., KRI-CREDIT-0001). Links the measurement to the KRI master registry entry.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7 Model Risk Management

description: Human-readable name of the KRI being measured (e.g., 'Non-Performing Loan Ratio', 'Liquidity Coverage Ratio', 'Value at Risk Utilisation'). Sourced from the KRI master registry.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance

description: Full name of the individual accountable for the KRI measurement, remediation, and escalation. Typically a senior risk officer or business unit head. Classified confidential as it identifies internal accountability assignments.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) Risk Governance Guidelines

description: The job title or role designation of the KRI owner (e.g., 'Chief Risk Officer', 'Head of Credit Risk', 'Treasury Risk Manager'). Supports role-based escalation routing and governance reporting.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Corporate Governance Principles for Banks

description: The business date on which the KRI value was observed or calculated. Represents the as-of date for the measurement, distinct from the record creation timestamp. Critical for time-series trend analysis and regulatory reporting periods.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Federal Reserve System (Fed) CCAR/DFAST Reporting Requirements

description: The prescribed frequency at which this KRI is measured and reported. Determines the cadence of data collection and dashboard refresh cycles (e.g., LCR is measured daily, NSFR monthly, ICAAP KRIs quarterly).
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Basel III LCR/NSFR Standards; Federal Reserve System (Fed) CCAR Annual Cycle

description: The end date of the measurement period over which the KRI value was calculated. For point-in-time KRIs this equals measurement_date; for flow-based KRIs this defines the close of the accumulation window.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) ASC 825

description: The start date of the measurement period over which the KRI value was calculated. For point-in-time KRIs this equals measurement_date; for flow-based KRIs (e.g., monthly loss events) this defines the beginning of the accumulation window.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Financial Accounting Standards Board (FASB) ASC 825

description: The line of business (LOB) or organisational unit responsible for managing and remediating this KRI (e.g., 'Commercial Lending', 'Treasury', 'Retail Banking', 'Trading'). Used for accountability assignment in GRC reporting and board risk committee packs.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) Risk Assessment System

description: The KRI actual value from the immediately preceding measurement period. Stored on the record to support period-over-period variance analysis and trend computation without requiring a self-join. Expressed in the same unit_of_measure as actual_value.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Federal Reserve System (Fed) CCAR/DFAST Reporting

description: The boundary defining the breach (red) zone for the KRI. Values exceeding this threshold indicate a material risk limit breach requiring immediate escalation to senior management or the board risk committee.
reference: Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) BCBS 239; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7

description: Free-text description of the management action plan or remediation steps being taken to address a KRI breach or amber warning. Captured for audit trail and board risk committee reporting purposes.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) Risk Assessment System

description: The target date by which the remediation action for a KRI breach must be completed. Used to track SLA compliance and flag overdue escalations in GRC dashboards.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7; Financial Stability Board (FSB) Risk Appetite Framework Guidance

description: High-level risk taxonomy category to which this KRI belongs. Aligns with the enterprise risk taxonomy and Basel III risk classification framework. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit|market|liquidity|operational|compliance|reputational|strategic — promote to reference product]
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Basel III Framework; Federal Reserve System (Fed) SR 11-7

description: Granular risk sub-classification within the risk category (e.g., 'counterparty credit risk', 'interest rate risk in the banking book', 'settlement risk', 'conduct risk'). Supports drill-down analysis in GRC reporting.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Basel III Framework; Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) Risk Assessment System

description: Indicates whether this measurement record represents a stress scenario value (True) rather than a base-case actual measurement (False). Stress scenario KRI readings are used in CCAR, DFAST, and ICAAP submissions.
reference: Federal Reserve System (Fed) CCAR/DFAST Stress Testing Requirements; Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Stress Testing Principles

description: Indicates the directionality of the KRI thresholds. 'higher_is_worse' means increasing values are adverse (e.g., NPL ratio, VaR). 'lower_is_worse' means decreasing values are adverse (e.g., LCR, CET1 ratio). 'range_bound' means both extremes are adverse.

description: The directional trend of the KRI value relative to the prior measurement period. 'improving' indicates movement toward lower risk, 'deteriorating' indicates movement toward higher risk, 'stable' indicates no material change. Used for forward-looking risk dashboard indicators.

description: The unit in which the KRI actual value is expressed (e.g., 'percentage', 'basis_points', 'USD', 'ratio', 'count', 'days'). Ensures correct interpretation of the actual_value field across heterogeneous KRI types.

business_glossary_term: Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review / Dodd-Frank Act Stress Testing (CCAR/DFAST) Applicable Indicator

description: Classification of the internal control failure that contributed to the operational risk event. 'design_failure' indicates the control was inadequately designed; 'operating_failure' indicates the control existed but did not operate effectively; 'no_control' indicates absence of a required control; 'override' indicates management override of controls; 'circumvention' indicates deliberate bypass of controls.
reference: SOX Section 404; COSO Internal Control Framework; OCC Supervisory Guidance; BCBS Operational Risk

description: Target completion date for the corrective action plan associated with this operational risk event. Used for management escalation tracking and regulatory examination readiness.
reference: OCC Supervisory Guidance; SOX Section 404; COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Status of the corrective action plan (CAP) initiated in response to the operational risk event to remediate the identified control failure. 'not_required' for near-miss events with no control gap; 'planned' for CAP defined but not started; 'in_progress' for active remediation; 'completed' for fully remediated; 'overdue' for past target completion date.
reference: OCC Supervisory Guidance on Operational Risk; SOX Section 404; COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Timestamp when the operational risk event record was first captured and persisted in the GRC platform or internal loss database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; BCBS Operational Risk Data Standards

description: The date on which the operational risk event was first identified or discovered by the bank, which may differ significantly from the event date for latent losses. Used to measure detection lag and assess control effectiveness.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices; Moodys RiskAuthority — Loss Event Capture

description: The date on which the operational risk loss event actually occurred or commenced. This is the principal real-world event date used for SMA capital charge period allocation and regulatory reporting. Distinct from discovery date and accounting date.
reference: BCBS Basel III — Standardized Measurement Approach (SMA); BCBS 196 Operational Risk Data Collection Exercise

description: Detailed narrative description of the operational risk event, including what happened, how it was identified, and the immediate impact. Supports root cause analysis, regulatory examination responses, and internal audit reviews.
reference: OCC Supervisory Guidance on Operational Risk; PRA SS1/23; SOX Section 404

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the operational risk event by the GRC platform or internal loss database (e.g., ORE-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reconciliation and regulatory reporting submissions to OCC/Fed/PRA.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices; OCC Bulletin 2019-37; PRA SS1/23

description: Current lifecycle status of the operational risk event record. 'open' indicates active loss event under investigation; 'under_review' indicates root cause analysis in progress; 'closed' indicates fully resolved and signed off; 'near_miss' indicates no financial loss but control failure identified; 'pending_recovery' indicates awaiting insurance or third-party recovery.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices; SAS Risk Management — Loss Event Workflow

description: Short, descriptive title summarizing the nature of the operational risk event (e.g., 'Unauthorized Wire Transfer — Treasury Operations', 'System Outage — Payment Processing Hub'). Used for management reporting and GRC dashboard display.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices; GRC Platform — Moodys RiskAuthority

description: Reference identifier linking this internal loss event to a corresponding record in an external operational risk loss data consortium (e.g., ORX — Operational Riskdata eXchange Association). Supports benchmarking of internal loss experience against industry peers.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices — Use of External Data; ORX Loss Data Standards

description: Total gross financial loss incurred from the operational risk event before any recoveries, expressed in the reporting currency. This is the primary input to the SMA capital charge calculation and must meet the BCBS minimum loss threshold for regulatory reporting (typically USD 20,000 for internal data collection).
reference: BCBS Basel III — SMA Capital Charge; BCBS 196 Operational Risk Data Collection; OCC Reporting

description: Portion of the total recovery amount attributable specifically to insurance policy payouts (e.g., fidelity bond, professional indemnity, cyber insurance). Tracked separately per BCBS guidance on insurance recognition in operational risk capital.
reference: BCBS Basel III — Insurance Recognition in Operational Risk; OCC Supervisory Guidance

description: Status of any legal proceedings or litigation associated with the operational risk event. 'none' indicates no legal action; 'threatened' indicates potential litigation notified; 'filed' indicates lawsuit filed; 'settled' indicates out-of-court settlement reached; 'judgment' indicates court judgment rendered; 'dismissed' indicates case dismissed.
reference: FASB ASC 450 Contingencies; IFRS IAS 37 Provisions; OCC Supervisory Guidance; SOX

description: Financial provision or reserve established in the general ledger for potential legal liability arising from the operational risk event. Recognized per IFRS IAS 37 or FASB ASC 450 when outflow is probable and estimable.
reference: IFRS IAS 37 Provisions Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets; FASB ASC 450; SOX Section 404

description: Date on which the recovery amount was received or recognized, whether from insurance, legal settlement, or third-party reimbursement. Used for cash flow timing analysis and SMA recovery component allocation.
reference: BCBS Basel III — SMA Recovery Component; BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices

description: Flag indicating whether the gross loss amount meets or exceeds the minimum loss data threshold (typically USD 20,000 per BCBS guidance) required for inclusion in the SMA Loss Component calculation and external loss data consortium submissions.
reference: BCBS Basel III — SMA; BCBS 196 Operational Risk Data Collection Exercise

description: Flag indicating whether this record represents a near-miss incident (potential loss that was averted) rather than an actual realized loss event. Near-miss events have zero gross loss but are captured for control environment assessment and KRI monitoring.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices; OCC Supervisory Guidance on Operational Risk

description: Net financial loss after deducting all recoveries from the gross loss amount (gross_loss_amount minus recovery_amount). This is the final economic impact figure used for P&L attribution, RAROC calculation, and management reporting.
reference: BCBS Basel III — SMA; IFRS 9; FASB ASC 450

description: Total amount recovered from all sources (insurance, legal settlements, third-party reimbursements, collateral liquidation) following the operational risk loss event. Subtracted from gross loss to derive net loss.
reference: BCBS Basel III — SMA; BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices; OCC Supervisory Guidance

description: Flag indicating whether this operational risk event meets the threshold or criteria requiring mandatory regulatory reporting to OCC, Federal Reserve, PRA, or other supervisory authorities (e.g., significant operational events, cyber incidents, large losses).
reference: OCC Supervisory Guidance; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; PRA SS1/23; BCBS Operational Risk

description: High-level root cause classification of the operational risk event following root cause analysis (RCA). Standard taxonomy: 'people' (human error, misconduct, inadequate training), 'process' (process failure, inadequate controls, procedural breach), 'system' (IT system failure, data quality, cybersecurity), 'external_event' (natural disaster, third-party failure, fraud by external party).
reference: BCBS Operational Risk Sound Practices; ISO 31000 Risk Management; OCC Supervisory Guidance

description: Detailed narrative of the root cause analysis findings, identifying the specific underlying cause(s) of the operational risk event. Supports corrective action planning, control remediation, and regulatory examination responses.
reference: OCC Supervisory Guidance on Operational Risk; PRA SS1/23; SOX Section 404

description: Classification of how this event contributes to the SMA capital charge calculation under Basel III. 'loss_component' indicates the event feeds the Loss Component (LC) of the SMA formula; 'recovery_component' indicates a recovery adjustment; 'timing_adjustment' indicates a multi-year loss requiring temporal allocation per BCBS guidance.
reference: BCBS Basel III — Standardized Measurement Approach (SMA) for Operational Risk; BCBS d355

description: Code identifying the originating operational system from which this loss event record was sourced (e.g., MOODYS_RISK_AUTHORITY, SAS_RISK_MGMT, GRC_PLATFORM, MANUAL_ENTRY). Used for data lineage tracking and ETL reconciliation in the Databricks lakehouse.
reference: BCBS Operational Risk Data Standards; SOX Section 404 — Data Lineage

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the operational risk event record, including updates to loss amounts, recovery amounts, root cause classification, or status changes. Supports audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; OCC Supervisory Guidance on Operational Risk

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each risk limit record in the operational risk limit registry. Primary key for the risk_limit data product. reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) — Basel III Framework; BCBS FRTB

The approved maximum exposure threshold expressed as an absolute monetary or notional amount in the limit currency. Represents the binding constraint against which utilization is measured. For VaR limits this is the maximum permissible loss; for credit limits this is the maximum Exposure at Default (EAD).
reference: BCBS Basel III; BCBS FRTB; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: Name or title of the governance body or individual who approved this risk limit (e.g., 'ALCO', 'Board Risk Committee', 'CRO', 'Head of Market Risk'). Required for governance audit trail and escalation chain documentation.

description: Date on which the risk limit was formally approved by the designated approval authority. Establishes the governance record for regulatory examination and internal audit purposes.

description: Prescribed remediation action protocol to be executed upon a breach event (e.g., 'Immediate escalation to CRO and ALCO; position reduction within 24 hours; SAR filing if AML threshold exceeded'). Codifies the breach governance framework for operational consistency.

description: Cumulative number of breach events recorded against this limit since its effective date. Used for trend analysis, KRI monitoring, and regulatory reporting on limit governance effectiveness.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the limit amount is denominated (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency limit consolidation and FX-adjusted utilization calculations.

description: The most recently calculated utilization of the limit expressed in the limit currency. Represents the current exposure measured on the utilization_basis (e.g., current VaR, current EAD, current RWA). Updated by the risk management platform on the monitoring frequency cycle.

description: Identifier of the trading desk or business unit responsible for the positions subject to this limit. Aligns with desk hierarchy in Murex/Calypso trading and order management systems. Required for FRTB desk-level capital attribution.

description: Calendar date on which the risk limit becomes binding and active for utilization monitoring. Aligns with the approval date or the start of the applicable risk period (e.g., fiscal quarter, regulatory reporting period).
reference: BCBS Basel III; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: Name or role title of the first-level escalation contact notified upon a limit warning or breach event (e.g., 'Head of Market Risk', 'Senior Credit Officer'). Defines the escalation chain for breach governance.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: Name or role title of the second-level escalation contact notified if the breach is not remediated within the prescribed timeframe (e.g., 'Chief Risk Officer', 'Board Risk Committee Chair'). Supports multi-tier breach governance.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance; BCBS Principles for Sound Stress Testing

description: Calendar date on which the risk limit ceases to be binding. Null for open-ended standing limits. Limits approaching expiry trigger renewal workflows in the governance framework. Supports regulatory capital constraint management under Basel III/FRTB.
reference: BCBS Basel III; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code identifying the geographic scope of the limit (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'DEU'). Used for country risk concentration limits and cross-border exposure management.
reference: ISO 3166-1; BCBS Basel III; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Country Risk Management Guidance

description: Indicates whether the limit enforces a hard stop (True) that prevents new transactions from being booked once the limit is reached, or a soft limit (False) that triggers alerts and escalation but does not block transactions. Critical for real-time limit monitoring integration with trading and order management systems.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance; BCBS FRTB

description: Date of the most recent breach event recorded against this limit. Null if no breach has occurred. Used for recency analysis in KRI dashboards and regulatory examination responses.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance; BCBS Principles for Sound Stress Testing

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the risk limit (e.g., 'CRED-CORP-001', 'MKT-VAR-DESK-FX-002'). Used in regulatory submissions, audit trails, and cross-system references. Aligns with the limit reference code maintained in SAS Risk Management / Moody's RiskAuthority.
reference: BCBS Basel III; OCC Risk Management Guidance; Federal Reserve SR 11-7

description: Free-text narrative describing the business rationale, scope, and any special conditions or exclusions applicable to this risk limit. Provides context for governance reviews, regulatory examinations, and audit inquiries.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance; SOX Section 302/404

description: Descriptive business name of the risk limit (e.g., 'FX Trading Desk Daily VaR Limit', 'Corporate Credit Concentration Limit — Technology Sector'). Used in dashboards, governance reports, and ALCO presentations.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Handbook — Risk Management

description: Current lifecycle state of the risk limit record. 'active' indicates the limit is in force and being monitored; 'suspended' indicates temporary hold pending review; 'breached' indicates the limit is currently in excess; 'pending_approval' indicates awaiting governance sign-off.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance; BCBS Principles for Sound Stress Testing

description: Classification of the risk limit by risk category. Drives applicable measurement methodology and regulatory capital treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit|market|liquidity|concentration|operational|counterparty|country|sector|settlement|intraday — promote to reference product]
reference: BCBS Basel III; BCBS FRTB; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: Standardized code identifying the Line of Business (LOB) responsible for managing and consuming this risk limit (e.g., 'IB-EQUITIES', 'RETAIL-LENDING', 'CORP-CREDIT', 'TREASURY'). Used for LOB-level risk appetite reporting and CCAR/DFAST segmentation.
reference: Federal Reserve CCAR/DFAST Guidance; OCC Risk Management Guidance; BCBS Basel III

description: Frequency at which utilization is recalculated and compared against the limit threshold (e.g., 'real_time' for trading desk VaR limits, 'daily' for credit concentration limits, 'monthly' for LCR/NSFR limits). Drives the monitoring schedule in the risk management platform.
reference: BCBS FRTB; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: Indicates whether this limit is directly tied to a regulatory capital constraint (True) under Basel III/FRTB (e.g., RWA limit, CET1 buffer limit, LCR minimum). Flags limits that require regulatory reporting upon breach.
reference: BCBS Basel III; BCBS FRTB; Federal Reserve CCAR/DFAST; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: Scheduled frequency at which the risk limit is formally reviewed and reaffirmed by the approving authority. Drives governance calendar and ALCO agenda scheduling.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance; BCBS Principles for Sound Stress Testing

description: The type of business entity to which this limit applies. Determines the dimension of risk aggregation (e.g., 'counterparty' for single-name credit limits, 'desk' for trading desk VaR limits, 'lob' for Line of Business concentration limits, 'geography' for country risk limits). reference: BCBS Basel III; BCBS FRTB; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this limit record originates (e.g., 'SAS-RISK', 'MOODYS-RA', 'MUREX', 'AXIOMSL'). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Federal Reserve SR 11-7; SOX Section 302/404; BCBS Basel III

description: Indicates whether this limit is a stress-scenario limit (True) applied under CCAR/DFAST stress testing frameworks, as opposed to a business-as-usual (BAU) limit. Stress limits typically have higher thresholds and different breach protocols.
reference: Federal Reserve CCAR/DFAST Guidance; BCBS Principles for Sound Stress Testing; Dodd-Frank Act Section 165

description: Further granular classification within the limit type (e.g., 'VaR', 'CVA', 'PV01', 'DV01', 'ECL', 'RWA', 'LCR', 'NSFR', 'Single-Name Concentration', 'Sector Concentration'). Enables drill-down analytics and precise regulatory mapping.
reference: BCBS FRTB; BCBS Basel III; Federal Reserve CCAR/DFAST Guidance

description: Timestamp when this risk limit record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks limit amendments, utilization updates, and status changes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity)

description: The precise date and time at which the current utilization amount was calculated or sourced from the risk management platform. Critical for intraday limit monitoring and audit trail integrity. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: BCBS FRTB; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance

description: The measurement methodology used to calculate current utilization against the limit. Determines which risk metric is compared to the limit amount (e.g., 'var' for Value at Risk limits, 'ead' for Exposure at Default credit limits, 'rwa' for Risk-Weighted Asset limits, 'lcr_outflow' for liquidity limits). description: Statistical confidence level used in the VaR calculation for market risk limits (e.g., 99.00 for 99th percentile, 97.50 for 97.5th percentile as required under FRTB Expected Shortfall). Applicable only for market risk limit types with VaR/ES utilization basis.
reference: BCBS FRTB; Federal Reserve SR 11-7; OCC Risk Management Guidance
