Date and time when the repayment transaction was approved for processing. Null for automated payments that do not require manual approval.
Identifier of the employee or system user who approved the repayment transaction, particularly for manual or exception-based payments. Used for audit and compliance.
Date and time when this repayment record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.
Number of days between the scheduled_payment_date and the actual payment_date. Positive values indicate late payment; negative values indicate early payment. Zero indicates on-time payment.
Foreign Exchange (FX) rate applied if the repayment currency differs from the loan currency. Null if no currency conversion occurred.
Portion of the payment allocated to fees such as servicing fees, processing fees, or prepayment fees. Excludes penalty interest.
Portion of the payment allocated to accrued interest charges on the loan. Contributes to Net Interest Income (NII) calculation.
Free-text field for additional comments or context about the repayment transaction. Used for customer service, dispute resolution, and audit purposes.
Remaining principal balance on the loan immediately after this repayment is applied. Critical for Exposure at Default (EAD) and credit risk calculations.
Total amount received in this repayment transaction, including principal, interest, fees, and penalties. Gross payment amount before allocation.
The date on which the repayment was received and applied to the loan account. This is the business event date for the repayment transaction.
The payment instrument or mechanism used to make the repayment. Includes Automated Clearing House (ACH), wire transfer, Real-Time Gross Settlement (RTGS), check, cash, or direct debit.
Identifier of the payment processing system or hub that handled this transaction. References systems such as ACI Worldwide or Volante payment platforms.
External payment reference number or transaction identifier provided by the payment system or customer. Used for reconciliation and customer inquiries.
Current processing status of the repayment transaction. Tracks lifecycle from receipt through application to the loan account.
Classification of whether the payment was received on-time, early, or late relative to the scheduled_payment_date. Critical for Non-Performing Loan (NPL) classification and delinquency tracking.

Classification of the repayment transaction type. Scheduled indicates a regular installment; prepayment indicates early principal reduction; partial indicates less than scheduled amount; balloon indicates large lump-sum; final indicates loan payoff.
Portion of the payment allocated to penalty interest charges for late payment or covenant breach. Tracked separately for regulatory reporting.
The date on which the repayment was posted to the General Ledger (GL) for accounting purposes. May differ from payment_date due to batch processing.
Indicates whether this repayment includes a prepayment component (principal paid ahead of schedule). True if prepayment occurred; false otherwise.
Portion of the payment allocated to reduce the outstanding principal balance of the loan. Core component for amortization tracking.
The date on which the repayment transaction was reversed, if applicable. Null if the transaction has not been reversed.
Indicates whether this repayment transaction has been reversed due to insufficient funds, dispute, or error. True if reversed; false otherwise.
Explanation for why the repayment was reversed, if applicable. Includes reasons such as insufficient funds, customer dispute, or processing error. Null if not reversed.
The originally scheduled due date for this repayment installment per the loan amortization schedule. Used to determine if payment is on-time, early, or late.
Precise date and time when the repayment transaction was processed by the payment system. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.
Date and time when this repayment record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes and ensuring data integrity.
The effective date from which the repayment amount is considered for interest calculation purposes. May differ from payment_date due to settlement timing.
Amount of fees or penalties waived by the bank as part of this repayment transaction. Used for customer relationship management and regulatory reporting.
Business justification for waiving fees or penalties, if applicable. Includes reasons such as customer goodwill, hardship, or error correction. Null if no waiver applied.
Unique identifier for the write-off event. Primary key for the write-off record.

Reference to the GL journal entry that recorded the write-off transaction, enabling reconciliation between loan system and financial accounting.

Reference to the borrower whose loan was written off.

The governance body or individual who approved the write-off: credit committee, chief credit officer, board of directors, or delegated authority per credit policy.

description: The date on which the write-off was formally approved by the designated authority.
The specific Call Report schedule and line item where this write-off is reported (e.g., Schedule RI-B Part II, Line 6a).
The primary reason for the charge-off: bankruptcy filing, borrower deceased, fraud, deemed uncollectable, statute of limitations expired, or other.
Free-text narrative providing additional context and details about the reason for the charge-off.
Indicates whether collateral was liquidated prior to or as part of the write-off process (True) or not (False).

description: The timestamp when this write-off record was first created in the system.
The total amount recovered from all sources (borrower payments, collateral liquidation, guarantor calls, debt sales) since the write-off date, updated with each recovery event.
The number of days the loan was past due at the time of write-off, used for delinquency analysis and LGD modeling.
The date on which the charged-off debt was sold to a third-party debt buyer, if applicable.
Indicates whether the charged-off debt was sold to a third-party debt buyer (True) or retained for internal collection (False).
The amount received from the sale of the charged-off debt to a third-party buyer, recorded as a recovery.
The total exposure at the time of default, used as the denominator in LGD calculation. Typically equals the total outstanding balance at default.

business_glossary_term: Expected Credit Loss (ECL) Reserve Released Amount
description: The amount of ECL (Expected Credit Loss) or ALLL (Allowance for Loan and Lease Losses) reserve released upon write-off, reducing the reserve balance.
The unpaid fees (late fees, penalty fees, servicing fees) that were charged off at the time of write-off.
The date the write-off transaction was posted to the general ledger, which may differ from the write-off date due to batch processing.
Indicates whether a guarantor was called upon for payment prior to or as part of the write-off process (True) or not (False).
The amount recovered from guarantor(s) prior to write-off, applied to reduce the write-off amount.
reference: Internal Credit Policy, Basel III LGD Framework

description: The accrued but unpaid interest that was charged off at the time of write-off.
The timestamp when this write-off record was last updated, capturing any changes to recovery amounts, status, or other fields.
The calculated Loss Given Default rate for this write-off: net loss after recovery divided by exposure at default (EAD). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4500 for 45%).
The net loss recognized after applying reserve release: total write-off amount minus ECL reserve released. This is the P&L impact of the charge-off.
The net loss remaining after all recoveries: total write-off amount minus cumulative recovery to date. Used for final LGD calculation.
The date the loan was first classified as non-performing (NPL), typically 90+ days past due, prior to write-off.
The outstanding principal balance of the loan that was charged off at the time of write-off.
Current status of post-charge-off recovery efforts: active pursuit, suspended, closed with no recovery, closed with partial recovery, or closed with full recovery.
The regulatory asset classification of the loan at the time of write-off per OCC/FDIC guidelines: substandard, doubtful, or loss.
Indicates whether this write-off must be included in regulatory net charge-off reporting to OCC, FDIC, or Federal Reserve (True) or is excluded (False).
The total amount written off, including principal, interest, and fees. Sum of all written-off components.
The date on which the loan balance was formally written off and removed from performing assets.
Externally visible business identifier for the write-off transaction, used for audit trails and regulatory reporting.
Current lifecycle status of the write-off record: pending approval, approved by credit committee, posted to general ledger, reversed due to error or recovery, or closed after final settlement.

Classification of the write-off event: full charge-off (entire balance), partial charge-off (portion of balance), technical write-off (accounting treatment without legal discharge), or regulatory write-off (mandated by regulator).

Indicates whether the guarantee contains an automatic extension clause. True if the guarantee automatically extends unless the issuing bank provides notice of non-renewal.

description: The full legal name of the issuer as registered with the incorporation authority. This is the official name used in legal documents, securities filings, and regulatory submissions.
reference: GLEIF LEI Standard, SEC Edgar Filing Requirements

description: Current long-term issuer rating assigned by Moody's Investors Service. Used for credit risk assessment, portfolio limits, and regulatory capital calculations.
reference: Moody's Rating Methodology, Basel III Framework

description: Date when the current Moody's issuer rating was assigned or last affirmed.
Moody's opinion on the likely direction of the issuer rating over the medium term.
ISO 10383 Market Identifier Code (MIC) for the issuer's primary listing exchange. Used for market data sourcing and trading venue identification.

Basel III regulatory classification determining capital treatment, risk weighting methodology, and large exposure limits. Financial institutions receive different treatment than non-financial corporates.
reference: Basel III Standardized Approach, CRR Article 4, Federal Reserve Regulation Q

description: Current long-term issuer credit rating assigned by Standard & Poor's. Used for credit risk assessment, RWA calculation under Standardized Approach, and investment policy compliance.
reference: S&P Global Ratings Methodology, Basel III Standardized Approach

description: Date when the current S&P issuer credit rating was assigned or last affirmed. Critical for determining rating staleness in credit risk models.
S&P's forward-looking opinion on the potential direction of the issuer credit rating over the intermediate term (typically 6-24 months).
Primary stock exchange ticker symbol for publicly traded issuers. Used for market data integration and equity price monitoring.
Timestamp when this issuer record was last modified. Critical for change data capture and downstream synchronization.
Official corporate website URL for the issuer. Used for investor relations access and ESG data sourcing.
reference: SEC Regulation FD, Corporate Governance Standards

description: Unique identifier for the exchange listing record.

Reference to the underlying security or financial instrument being listed.

Foreign key linking to reference.jurisdiction. Business justification: Exchange listings are subject to jurisdiction-specific securities regulations (prospectus requirements, short-selling rules, MiFID II liquidity classification). Compliance and trading teams need the l

Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Trading currency determines order entry and execution pricing. Distinct from settlement currency for dual-listed securities. Essential for FX risk management and multi-currency trading desk operations
Price movement threshold percentage that triggers automatic trading halt or circuit breaker for this listing.
Timestamp when this listing record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether this is the current active version of the listing record in a slowly changing dimension (SCD) Type 2 history.
Date when the security was delisted or removed from trading on this exchange. Null if currently listed.
Timestamp from which this version of the listing record is effective, supporting slowly changing dimension (SCD) Type 2 historization.
Timestamp until which this version of the listing record is effective. Null indicates current active record.
Exchange-specific ticker or trading symbol used to identify the security on this particular exchange.
Annual or one-time fee charged by the exchange for maintaining this listing, in the listing currency.
ISO 6166 twelve-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the security globally. Included here for denormalized reference and regulatory reporting convenience.
Final date on which the security can be traded on this exchange before expiry or delisting. Applicable primarily to derivatives and structured products.
Date when the security was first listed and became available for trading on this exchange.

Current trading status of the listing on the exchange. Active indicates normal trading; suspended indicates temporary halt due to regulatory or corporate action; halted indicates intraday trading pause; delisted indicates removed from exchange; pending indicates listing application in progress.

Classification of the listing relationship. Primary indicates home exchange; secondary indicates additional listing; dual indicates simultaneous primary listing on multiple exchanges; ADR indicates American Depositary Receipt; GDR indicates Global Depositary Receipt.
Exchange-specific instrument identifier or code assigned by the listing exchange for internal reference and order routing.
Minimum trading unit or board lot size for this listing. Orders must be in multiples of this quantity.
Exchange-defined tier or package level for market data distribution rights for this listing (e.g., Level 1, Level 2, Premium).
Indicates whether designated market makers or liquidity providers are required for this listing under exchange rules.

business_glossary_term: Markets in Financial Instruments Directive (MiFID) II Liquid Flag
description: Indicates whether this listing is classified as a liquid instrument under MiFID II transparency and best execution requirements.
Lower limit percentage for intraday price movement relative to reference price, beyond which orders are rejected or trading is paused.
Upper limit percentage for intraday price movement relative to reference price, beyond which orders are rejected or trading is paused.
Multiplier applied to quoted price to calculate actual contract or notional value. Commonly used for derivatives and structured products.
Indicates whether this is the primary or home exchange listing for the security. True for primary listing; false for secondary or cross-listings.
Indicates whether trades in this listing must be reported to regulatory authorities under MiFIR, EMIR, or equivalent transaction reporting regimes.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which trades are settled for this listing. May differ from trading currency for certain cross-listed instruments.
Number of business days after trade date (T+N) for standard settlement of trades in this listing. Common values are T+1 or T+2.
Indicates whether short selling is permitted for this listing under current exchange and regulatory rules.
Name of the financial institution or broker-dealer sponsoring or facilitating the listing on this exchange.
Minimum price increment or tick size for this listing. Prices must move in multiples of this value.
Local exchange time when regular trading session ends for this listing, in HH:MM format.
Local exchange time when regular trading session begins for this listing, in HH:MM format.
Current intraday trading phase or session state for this listing on the exchange.
IANA timezone identifier for the exchange trading hours (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo).

Classification of the trading venue under MiFID II. Regulated Market indicates traditional exchange; MTF indicates Multilateral Trading Facility; OTF indicates Organised Trading Facility; Systematic Internaliser indicates bank internal crossing.
Timestamp when this listing record was last modified.
Unique identifier for the yield curve record. Primary key.

Workflow approval state for the curve. Approved curves are authorized for use in production valuation and risk systems.
Date and time when the curve was approved for production use. Supports audit and compliance reporting.
User ID or name of the market risk officer or quant who approved the curve for production use.
Collateral arrangement assumed for derivative discounting (e.g., OIS-collateralized for CSA trades, uncollateralized for legacy trades).

description: Precise date and time when the yield curve was constructed or last recalculated, supporting intraday curve updates and audit trails.

Credit quality classification for credit spread curves (e.g., AAA, AA, A, BBB). Not applicable for government or risk-free curves.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Standardized Approach

description: Business name or label for the yield curve (e.g., USD SOFR Swap Curve, EUR Government Curve, USD Corporate AA Spread Curve).

JSON-serialized array of tenor-rate nodes defining the complete curve shape. Each node includes tenor label, tenor in days, zero rate, par rate, forward rate, discount factor, and interpolation method. Supports full curve reconstruction for valuation and risk analytics.
Name of the stress or shock scenario applied to the curve (e.g., +100 bps parallel shift, CCAR Severely Adverse). Null for base case curves.
reference: Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR), Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS)

description: Current lifecycle status of the yield curve. Active curves are used for valuation; superseded curves are retained for historical analysis.

Classification of the yield curve by underlying instrument or market segment. Determines the curve's application in valuation and risk measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: government|swap|credit_spread|ois|corporate|treasury|libor|sofr|euribor — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Market Risk Framework

description: Version number for the curve construction on a given date. Supports intraday curve revisions and audit trails.
Day count basis used for calculating accrued interest and discount factors (e.g., ACT/360, ACT/365, 30/360). Aligns with market conventions for the curve's instrument class.
reference: ISDA Definitions, International Swaps and Derivatives Association (ISDA)

description: Date from which the curve is valid for valuation purposes. Typically aligns with construction_date but may differ for forward-starting curves.
Date after which the curve is no longer valid for valuation. Null for curves that remain active until superseded.
Technique applied to interpolate rates between observed tenor nodes on the curve. Critical for pricing instruments with non-standard maturities.
Flag indicating whether the curve represents a risk-free rate (e.g., government or OIS curve). True for risk-free curves, false for credit or spread curves.
reference: Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS) Market Risk Framework

description: Tenor in days of the longest maturity node on the curve (e.g., 10950 for 30 years). Defines the curve's maximum extrapolation horizon.
Vendor or exchange providing the underlying market instrument prices used to construct the curve (e.g., Bloomberg, Refinitiv, ICE, CME).
Regulatory standard or stress scenario applied to the curve (e.g., CCAR Severely Adverse, DFAST, IFRS 9 Stage 2). Applicable for regulatory reporting curves.
reference: Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR), Dodd-Frank Act Stress Testing (DFAST)

description: Industry sector for corporate or credit spread curves (e.g., financials, industrials, utilities). Supports sector-specific risk analysis.
Tenor in days of the shortest maturity node on the curve (e.g., 1 for overnight, 30 for 1-month).
Name of the trading or risk management system that constructed the curve (e.g., Murex, Calypso, Bloomberg, internal curve engine).
Number of tenor nodes (pillars) defining the curve shape. Typical curves have 8-15 nodes spanning overnight to 30 years.
Date and time when the yield curve record was last modified. Tracks changes to curve metadata or node data.
Primary business use case for the curve (e.g., trading book mark-to-market, IFRS 9 ECL discounting, FTP curve, regulatory stress testing).
reference: International Financial Reporting Standard 9 (IFRS 9), Basel Committee on Banking Supervision (BCBS)

Any discount or reduction applied to the gross fee amount. May result from promotional offers, relationship pricing, fee waivers for underperformance, or negotiated concessions for high-net-worth clients.

The total fee amount calculated before any adjustments, discounts, or tax withholding. Calculated as AUM basis amount multiplied by the applied fee rate (prorated if necessary).

description: The date the invoice was generated and sent to the client. Establishes the start of the payment due period per the client service agreement.

The date and time when this fee billing record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.

The final fee amount charged to the client after applying discounts and tax withholding. This is the amount that will be debited from the client portfolio or invoiced. Calculated as gross fee amount minus discount amount minus tax withholding amount.

The date on which the fee revenue is recognized in the financial statements. For management fees, this is typically the end of the billing period. For performance fees, recognition may be deferred until the performance measurement period concludes.

description: The date the fee billing was reversed. Triggers creation of offsetting general ledger entries and refund processing if payment was already received.

Identifier of the source system that generated this fee billing record. Typically the wealth and portfolio management system such as SimCorp Dimension or BlackRock Aladdin. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

The amount of tax withheld from the fee, if applicable. Certain jurisdictions require withholding tax on investment management fees, particularly for non-resident clients or cross-border advisory services.

Status of fee accrual for revenue recognition purposes. Accruing indicates fees are being accumulated daily; accrued indicates the period is complete and ready for recognition.
The total Assets Under Management value used as the basis for fee calculation in this billing period. May be average daily balance, beginning balance, or ending balance depending on agreement terms.
The method used to determine the AUM basis for fee calculation. Average daily is most precise; beginning or ending balance is simpler but less accurate during volatile periods.
The cadence at which fees are calculated and billed to the client. Monthly and quarterly are most common for management fees; performance fees are typically annual or semi-annual.
Indicates whether fees are billed at the beginning (true) or end (false) of the billing period. Management fees are commonly billed in advance; performance fees are billed in arrears.

Method by which the fee is calculated. AUM percentage applies a rate to total assets, flat is a fixed amount, tiered uses breakpoints with different rates per tier, performance-based links to investment returns, transaction-based charges per trade, and hybrid combines multiple methods.

description: The primary risk category for RWA calculation and capital allocation. Determines how this profit center's activities contribute to the bank's total risk-weighted assets under Basel III.

description: Indicates whether this profit center is subject to SOX financial controls and requires enhanced documentation, segregation of duties, and audit trails. True for profit centers with material financial impact.

Unique identifier for the accounting period record. Primary key.
Reference to the fiscal calendar definition that governs this accounting period. Supports multiple fiscal calendars for different legal entities or reporting requirements.

description: Reference to the legal entity for which this accounting period is defined. Supports multi-entity consolidation and statutory reporting.
Free-text description or notes about the accounting period. May include special instructions, closing notes, or period-specific context.

Indicates whether this is an adjustment period (True) or a standard operating period (False). Adjustment periods (e.g., Period 13, Period 14) are used for year-end closing entries and do not correspond to calendar months.
Indicates whether this period is used for budgeting and planning (True) or is actuals-only (False). Supports budget vs. actual variance analysis.
reference: Oracle Financials Cloud Budgetary Control, SAP S/4HANA Budget Management

description: Defines the order in which periods should be closed in a multi-period close process. Lower numbers close first. Supports controlled, sequential period-end closing workflows.

description: The date on which the accounting period was closed. Null if the period is still open or has never been closed. Supports audit trail and period lifecycle tracking.

reference: FASB ASC 810, IFRS 10, Oracle Financials Cloud Consolidation

description: The fiscal year to which this accounting period belongs (e.g., 2024, 2025). May differ from calendar year depending on the organization's fiscal calendar.

description: The date on which the accounting period was opened for posting. Supports audit trail and period lifecycle tracking.

description: Human-readable name of the accounting period, typically in the format 'MMM-YY' (e.g., 'JAN-24', 'FEB-24') or 'Period 1', 'Period 2'. Used for display and reporting purposes.

description: Current lifecycle status of the accounting period. Controls whether journal entries can be posted. 'future_enterable' allows posting to future periods; 'open' allows normal posting; 'closed' prevents posting but can be reopened; 'permanently_closed' locks the period for SOX compliance; 'never_opened' indicates the period has not yet been activated.

description: Classification of the accounting period. 'standard' for regular monthly periods; 'adjustment' for mid-year or quarterly adjustment periods; 'year_end' for annual closing and adjustment entries; 'opening' for the initial period when opening balances are recorded.

Indicates whether this period is included in external financial reporting (True) or is for internal management purposes only (False). Supports GAAP and IFRS reporting requirements.

description: The date on which the period was certified for SOX compliance. Null if not yet certified. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.

Indicates whether this period is the final period of the fiscal year (True) or not (False). Used to trigger year-end closing processes, retained earnings rollover, and annual financial statement preparation.

A detailed description of the purpose, usage guidelines, and business rules for this account. Provides additional context beyond the account name for users posting journal entries and for auditors reviewing account activity.

The level of this account in the chart of accounts hierarchy (e.g., 1 for top-level summary accounts, 2 for sub-categories, 3 for detail accounts). Used for drill-down reporting and financial statement presentation at different levels of detail.

The current lifecycle status of the general ledger account. Active accounts accept postings; inactive accounts are closed and do not accept new postings; blocked accounts are temporarily suspended; pending closure accounts are in the process of being closed after final reconciliation.

The fundamental accounting classification of the account determining its placement in the financial statements and its normal balance direction. Asset and expense accounts have debit normal balances; liability, equity, and revenue accounts have credit normal balances.

An alternate or legacy account code used for cross-reference purposes during system migrations, mergers and acquisitions (M&A), or for mapping to external reporting frameworks. Allows historical data to be linked to the current chart of accounts structure.

Identifies the primary financial statement where this account is reported (balance sheet for assets/liabilities/equity, income statement for revenue/expenses, cash flow statement for cash movements, statement of changes in equity for equity movements).

The functional area or business process this account supports (e.g., 'Commercial Lending', 'Investment Banking', 'Treasury', 'Risk Management', 'Operations', 'Technology'). Used for functional cost allocation and activity-based costing.

description: Reference to the chart of accounts template from which this account is instantiated. The COA defines the account structure and hierarchy.
reference: FASB Accounting Standards Codification, IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Reference to the cost center or responsibility center to which this account is assigned. Enables management accounting, budgeting, and performance analysis by organizational unit.
reference: Management Accounting Best Practices, SAP Controlling (CO) Module

description: Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: GL accounts track balances in specific currencies; this link enables currency-specific balance reporting, revaluation processing, and multi-currency consolidation required for IFRS and US GAAP complia
Reference to the legal entity under which this GL account is instantiated. A GL account is specific to a legal entity context for regulatory and consolidation purposes.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, GAAP ASC 810

description: Reference to the parent account in the account hierarchy. Enables roll-up reporting and hierarchical financial analysis.
reference: Oracle Financials Account Hierarchy, SAP GL Account Group

description: Foreign key linking to reference.product_type. Business justification: Banks map GL accounts to product types for FINREP and COREP regulatory reporting, enabling automated regulatory template population. A banking regulatory reporting expert would expect GL accounts to b
Reference to the profit center for internal profitability analysis. Used for segment reporting and management decision-making.
reference: IFRS 8 Operating Segments, SAP Profit Center Accounting

description: Sub-classification within the account type (e.g., current asset, fixed asset, operating expense, cost of goods sold). Used for financial statement line item grouping and regulatory reporting.
reference: IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, Basel III Pillar 3 Disclosure

description: The unique alphanumeric code identifying this account within the chart of accounts. Typically structured (e.g., segment-based) to encode account type, department, and classification.
reference: Oracle Financials Account Flexfield, SAP S/4HANA GL Account Number

description: Extended description of the account purpose, usage guidelines, and posting rules. Provides detailed context for accounting staff and auditors.
The level of this account in the chart of accounts hierarchy (1=top level, increasing for detail accounts). Used for reporting aggregation and drill-down.
The descriptive name of the GL account, providing human-readable identification of the account purpose (e.g., Cash and Cash Equivalents, Accounts Receivable - Trade).
The current operational status of the GL account. Inactive or closed accounts do not accept new postings but retain historical balances.
reference: Oracle Financials Account Lifecycle, SAP GL Account Master

description: Granular classification within the account category for detailed financial analysis and management reporting (e.g., trade receivables, employee receivables, intercompany receivables).
The fundamental accounting classification of the account: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense. Determines financial statement presentation and normal balance (debit/credit).
reference: FASB Conceptual Framework, IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: An alternative account code used for external reporting, regulatory filings, or legacy system integration. Supports cross-reference and mapping requirements.
Indicates whether postings to this account are subject to budget availability checking. Prevents overspending and enforces fiscal discipline.
reference: Oracle Financials Budget Control, SAP Funds Management

description: The date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The date on which this GL account becomes inactive and no longer accepts postings. Null for accounts with no planned end date.
The date from which this GL account becomes active and available for posting. Supports temporal account management and reorganizations.
The financial statement line item to which this account rolls up for external reporting (e.g., Cash and Cash Equivalents, Property Plant and Equipment).
reference: IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, GAAP ASC 210

description: Indicates whether this account is used for intercompany transactions requiring elimination during consolidation. Critical for group financial reporting.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, GAAP ASC 810

description: The user ID or name of the person who last modified this GL account record. Critical for change tracking and accountability.
The date and time when this GL account record was last modified. Enables change detection and audit trail reconstruction.
The monetary threshold above which variances or adjustments to this account require additional review or approval. Used for risk-based controls and audit scoping.
The normal balance side for this account: debit for assets and expenses, credit for liabilities, equity, and revenue. Used for balance validation and error detection.
Indicates whether direct journal entries can be posted to this account. Summary or control accounts typically do not allow direct posting.
reference: Oracle Financials Account Attributes, SAP GL Account Master

description: The required frequency for account reconciliation: daily for high-volume transaction accounts, monthly for standard accounts, quarterly for low-activity accounts.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, Internal Audit Standards

description: Indicates whether this account requires periodic reconciliation to external statements or subledger detail. Key control for SOX compliance and audit readiness.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Indicates whether this account is subject to foreign exchange revaluation or fair value remeasurement at period end. Applies to foreign currency accounts and financial instruments.
reference: IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates, IFRS 9 Financial Instruments

description: Indicates whether this account is subject to SOX internal control testing and documentation requirements. High-risk accounts require enhanced controls and audit trails.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: Indicates whether this is a statistical (non-monetary) account used for tracking quantities, headcount, or other non-financial metrics for allocation and analysis purposes.
reference: Management Accounting Best Practices, SAP Statistical Key Figures

description: Indicates whether this account is used for tax-related transactions (VAT, sales tax, withholding tax). Subject to specific tax reporting and compliance requirements.
Indicates whether this account is a control account for a subledger (AP, AR, fixed assets). Balance must reconcile to subledger detail.
reference: Oracle Financials Subledger Accounting, SAP Subledger Integration

description: The user ID or name of the person who created this GL account record. Part of the audit trail for account master data changes.
Unique system identifier for the general ledger journal entry header record.

Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: journal_entry stores accounting_period as STRING but should FK to accounting_period table. Journal entries are posted to specific accounting periods and need referential integrity to the period master

Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Journal entries are denominated in transaction currencies; this link is required for FX gain/loss calculation, multi-currency reporting, and currency translation per IAS 21 in banking operations.

Foreign key linking to reference.exchange_rate. Business justification: SOX and IFRS audit trails require journal entries to reference the exact exchange rate record used for multi-currency posting and FX revaluation. A banking auditor would expect this FK to validate tha
Reference to the legal entity (subsidiary, branch, operating company) to which this journal entry belongs for consolidation and statutory reporting.
reference: FASB ASC 810, IFRS 10, Basel III Pillar 3

description: Reference to the original journal entry that this entry reverses, establishing the reversal relationship for audit trail. Null if not a reversal.

Foreign key linking to ledger.subledger. Business justification: journal_entry has subledger_source_system (STRING) and subledger_transaction_reference (STRING) as text-based audit trail fields. Adding subledger_id FK to the subledger registry establishes a proper
The effective accounting date (transaction date) on which the journal entry is recognized in the general ledger for financial reporting purposes.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a period-end or year-end adjustment (true) or a regular operational entry (false), supporting financial close process tracking.
Current approval workflow status of the journal entry: draft (not submitted), pending approval (awaiting review), approved (ready for posting), or rejected (returned to preparer).
reference: SOX Section 404, COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Date and time when the journal entry was approved by the designated approver, supporting audit trail and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404, COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Full name of the user who approved the journal entry, for audit trail and accountability.
reference: SOX Section 404, COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Descriptive name of the batch or import run that created this journal entry, for operational tracking and troubleshooting.
Date and time when the journal entry record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SOX Section 404, COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Foreign exchange rate applied to translate entry currency amounts to functional currency, if applicable. Null for entries in functional currency.
ISO 4217 three-letter code for the functional currency of the legal entity, used for currency translation and consolidation.
Total credit amount translated into the functional currency of the legal entity using the applicable exchange rate at accounting date.
Total debit amount translated into the functional currency of the legal entity using the applicable exchange rate at accounting date.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction requiring elimination during consolidation (true) or is a single-entity transaction (false).
User-defined or system-assigned category for grouping and reporting journal entries (e.g., payroll, depreciation, revenue recognition, tax provision, consolidation).
Free-text narrative description of the business purpose and nature of the journal entry, supporting audit trail and user understanding.
Business-facing unique journal entry number assigned by the GL system or user, used for external reference and audit trail.

Origin or type of journal entry indicating how it was created: manual entry by user, automated posting from subledger (AP/AR/FA), intercompany elimination, period-end accrual, foreign exchange or fair value revaluation, reversal of prior entry, recurring template-based entry, account reclassification, top-side consolidation adjustment, or statistical (non-monetary) entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manual|subledger|intercompany|accrual|revaluation|reversal|recurring|reclassification|top_side_adjustment|statistical — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FASB ASC 105-10, IFRS IAS 1, SOX Section 404

description: Date and time when the journal entry record was last updated, supporting change tracking and audit trail.
reference: SOX Section 404, COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Current posting status of the journal entry in the general ledger: unposted (not yet affecting GL balances), posted (committed to GL), reversed (original entry reversed by a subsequent entry), or voided (cancelled before posting).
Date and time when the journal entry was posted to the general ledger, marking the point at which it affects account balances and financial statements.
Full name of the user who prepared the journal entry, for audit trail and accountability.
reference: SOX Section 404, COSO Internal Control Framework

description: The accounting date on which this journal entry will be or was automatically reversed, typically used for accrual entries. Null if not a reversing entry.
Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a prior entry (true) or an original entry (false).

Reference code or identifier linking this journal entry to the applicable SOX internal control (e.g., control ID for manual journal entry review, approval workflow, or segregation of duties), supporting Section 302 and 404 compliance.
reference: SOX Section 302, SOX Section 404, COSO Internal Control Framework

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is statistical (non-monetary, used for ratio calculations and management reporting) rather than a financial accounting entry.
Name or code of the upstream subledger system (AP, AR, FA, Payroll, etc.) that originated this journal entry, supporting data lineage and reconciliation.
Unique transaction identifier from the source subledger system, enabling drill-back and reconciliation between GL and subledger.
Sum of all credit line amounts in the journal entry, expressed in the entry currency. Must equal total_debit_amount for a balanced entry.
Sum of all debit line amounts in the journal entry, expressed in the entry currency. Must equal total_credit_amount for a balanced entry.
Unique identifier for the journal entry line. Primary key for atomic GL postings.

Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: journal_entry_line stores accounting_period as STRING but should FK to accounting_period. While journal_entry_line already FKs to journal_entry (which will FK to accounting_period), line-level period

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: GL entries must track originating channel for regulatory reporting (CTR/SAR filings require channel identification), channel profitability analysis, and transaction source attribution. Standard in ret
FK to ledger.cost_center

Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Individual journal lines may be entered in currencies different from header; this link supports multi-currency transaction processing, line-level FX calculation, and detailed currency reporting in ban

Foreign key linking to reference.exchange_rate. Business justification: Each journal entry line in a multi-currency transaction must reference the specific exchange rate record for FX P&L attribution, revaluation audit trails, and regulatory reporting. Banking controllers
FK to ledger.gl_account

Foreign key linking to security.instrument. Business justification: Securities transactions (purchases, sales, mark-to-market adjustments, coupon accruals) post to GL at line level. IFRS 9, Basel III regulatory capital, and investment accounting require instrument-lev
Reference to the parent journal entry header. Links this line to the batch-level posting event.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Journal entry lines reference the source payment transaction for subledger-to-GL reconciliation, audit trail, and regulatory reporting. Banks need to trace each journal line back to the originating pa
FK to ledger.profit_center
Reference to the original journal entry line being reversed. Maintains audit trail for reversing entries.

Foreign key linking to ledger.subledger. Business justification: journal_entry_line has subledger_reference_code (STRING) and subledger_type (STRING) as text-based references to the originating subledger. Adding subledger_id FK to the subledger registry normalizes

Type of accounting adjustment represented by this line. Supports financial close analytics and SOX control testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|accrual|reclassification|elimination|revaluation|provision|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Business segment or division code for segment reporting. Supports IFRS 8 and regulatory segment disclosures.
Timestamp when this journal entry line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
Credit amount in functional currency. Represents increases to liability, equity, and revenue accounts or decreases to asset and expense accounts.
Debit amount in functional currency. Represents increases to asset and expense accounts or decreases to liability, equity, and revenue accounts.
Business effective date of the transaction. May differ from posting date for backdated or forward-dated entries.
Transaction amount in the original entered currency before conversion to functional currency. Supports multi-currency operations and FX analysis.
Exchange rate applied to convert entered currency to functional currency. Supports FX revaluation and CVA calculations.
Type of exchange rate used for currency conversion. Supports different rate types for various accounting treatments.
Fiscal year to which this posting belongs. Supports annual financial reporting and multi-year analysis.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the functional currency of the posting entity. Typically USD for US-based banks.
Legal entity or intercompany counterparty code for consolidation and intercompany eliminations. Critical for group-level IFRS/GAAP consolidation.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this journal entry line. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements.
Detailed description of the journal entry line. Provides business context and audit trail for the posting.
Sequential line number within the journal entry. Establishes ordering for audit trail and display purposes.
Date on which the journal entry line was posted to the general ledger. Critical for financial reporting cutoff and period assignment.
Current posting status of the journal entry line. Supports financial close workflow and SOX controls.
Project or initiative code for capital expenditure tracking and project accounting. Supports investment banking deal tracking and infrastructure projects.
Indicates whether this line is a reversal of a prior posting. Supports accrual reversals and error corrections.
Code identifying the originating system. Examples include T24, MUREX, CALYPSO, SAP, or MANUAL for manual entries.
Non-monetary statistical amount for allocation or reporting purposes. Supports headcount, square footage, or other allocation bases.
Unit of measure for the statistical amount. Examples include headcount, square meters, or transaction count.

Type of subledger that originated this posting. Links GL to operational systems such as AP, AR, loan origination, or trading platforms. Tax code for VAT, GST, or other tax treatment. Supports tax reporting and compliance with FATCA and CRS requirements.
Unique identifier for the trial balance record. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the accounting period (month, quarter, year) for which this trial balance snapshot is captured. Supports period-end close and financial reporting cycles.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, FASB ASC 205

description: Reference to the cost center or business unit dimension for management accounting and internal reporting. May be null for balance sheet accounts not allocated to cost centers.
reference: Management Accounting Standards, Oracle Financials / SAP S/4HANA Cost Center Accounting

description: Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Trial balance reports are currency-specific; this link enables accurate currency-specific balance reporting and supports multi-currency trial balance preparation for financial close.

Foreign key linking to reference.exchange_rate. Business justification: Period-end trial balance revaluation requires linking to the specific approved exchange rate used — mandatory for IFRS/GAAP financial close, external audit sign-off, and regulatory reporting (FINREP).
Reference to the chart of accounts GL account for which this balance is reported. Links to the account master defining account type, category, and financial statement line item.
reference: Oracle Financials / SAP S/4HANA Chart of Accounts

description: Foreign key linking to security.instrument. Business justification: Trial balance reporting by instrument supports portfolio valuation, investment performance measurement, and regulatory capital calculations. Required for IFRS 9 expected credit loss modeling, Basel II
Reference to the legal entity for which this trial balance is prepared. Critical for consolidation and regulatory reporting under IFRS/GAAP.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, FASB ASC 810

Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this trial balance line includes post-close adjustments, reclassifications, or audit adjustments. Supports SOX audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: Date on which the trial balance or account reconciliation was approved. Critical for financial close timeline tracking and SOX audit trail.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: User ID or name of the individual who approved the trial balance or account reconciliation. Supports maker-checker controls and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: Current status of the trial balance record in the financial close cycle. Indicates whether the balance is draft (pre-close), preliminary (soft close), final (hard close), audited (external audit complete), or adjusted (post-close adjustment).
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: Classification of the trial balance record indicating whether it represents actual posted transactions, budgeted amounts, forecasted amounts, adjusted balances, restated balances for prior period corrections, or pro forma balances for scenario analysis.
reference: IFRS IAS 1, FASB ASC 205, Management Accounting Standards

description: Ending credit balance for this GL account at the end of the accounting period. Calculated as opening balance plus period activity. Forms the basis for financial statement preparation.
Ending debit balance for this GL account at the end of the accounting period. Calculated as opening balance plus period activity. Forms the basis for financial statement preparation.
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this trial balance line has been marked for elimination in the consolidation process. Used to remove intercompany balances and transactions.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, FASB ASC 810

description: Exchange rate used to convert transaction currency amounts to functional currency. May be spot rate, average rate, or historical rate depending on the nature of the GL account per IAS 21.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Type of exchange rate applied for currency conversion. Spot rate for monetary items, average rate for income statement items, historical rate for non-monetary items, closing rate for balance sheet translation.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Timestamp when this trial balance record was extracted from the source system and loaded into the data lakehouse. Supports data lineage and freshness tracking.
Closing credit balance converted to the functional currency of the legal entity. Used for consolidated financial statements and regulatory reporting.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Closing debit balance converted to the functional currency of the legal entity. Used for consolidated financial statements and regulatory reporting.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional currency of the legal entity as defined under IAS 21. Used for consolidation and reporting.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates, ISO 4217

description: Opening credit balance converted to the functional currency of the legal entity using the appropriate exchange rate. Used for consolidation and group reporting.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Opening debit balance converted to the functional currency of the legal entity using the appropriate exchange rate. Used for consolidation and group reporting.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Period credit activity converted to the functional currency of the legal entity. Supports multi-currency consolidation and reporting under IFRS/GAAP.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Period debit activity converted to the functional currency of the legal entity. Supports multi-currency consolidation and reporting under IFRS/GAAP.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this trial balance line includes intercompany transactions that require elimination during consolidation under IFRS 10 / FASB ASC 810.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, FASB ASC 810

description: Free-text field for capturing explanatory notes, reconciliation comments, or audit observations related to this trial balance line. Supports documentation requirements for financial close and audit.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: Credit balance brought forward from the prior accounting period for this GL account. Represents the beginning balance for the current period.
Debit balance brought forward from the prior accounting period for this GL account. Represents the beginning balance for the current period.
Total credit transactions posted to this GL account during the current accounting period. Includes journal entries from all subledgers and manual adjustments.
Total debit transactions posted to this GL account during the current accounting period. Includes journal entries from all subledgers and manual adjustments.
User ID or name of the individual who performed the account reconciliation. Supports segregation of duties and audit trail requirements under SOX.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: Date on which the account reconciliation was completed and approved. Critical for SOX compliance and audit trail documentation.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: Status of the account reconciliation process for this GL account and period. Tracks whether the balance has been reconciled to subledgers, supporting documentation, and external statements per SOX requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls, PCAOB AS 2201

description: Name or identifier of the source system from which this trial balance data was extracted (e.g., Oracle Financials, SAP S/4HANA, subledger system). Supports data lineage and audit trail.
